Voie du bastion|0|desc:
The PC grants their shield's DEF Mod. to a companion of their choice standing right next to them (they also keep this bonus for themselves). They may change companion each turn during their initiative phase. This is a free action. 
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC can protect two companions at the same time, provided both are standing right next to them.
Voie du bastion|1|desc:
During their turn, the PC takes only an attack or movement action. Afterwards, at any point before their next turn, the PC may make a melee attack roll in opposition to a successful melee or ranged attack from an opponent. On success, the opponent's attack is blocked by the shield.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the roll to block the attack gains a +2 bonus.
Voie du bastion|2|desc: The PC may make a shield attack each turn using a d12 instead of a d20 (free action) that deals [1d4 + STR Mod.] DMG.
Voie du bastion|3|desc: During their turn, the PC takes only an attack or movement action. Afterwards, at any point before their next turn, the PC may make a magic attack roll (INT Mod.) in opposition to a magic attack roll targeting them. On success, the spell is absorbed by the shield and has no effect on the PC.
Voie du bastion|4|desc: The PC can wear plate armor. It grants a DEF of +7 and protects against critical hits (on a critical, the PC takes normal DMG instead of doubled DMG).
Voie de la bravoure|0|desc:
Upon taking this ability, the PC gains 3 additional HP at rank 1, then 3 more HP upon reaching rank 3 in this path, and finally 3 HP at rank 5.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the HP bonus increases to 4.
Voie de la bravoure|1|desc:
The PC has hardened their body. They gain a +2 bonus to DEF.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the DEF bonus increases by +1.
Voie de la bravoure|2|desc: The PC often accomplishes extraordinary physical feats. Once per turn, you may sacrifice 1d4 HP to gain +5 on a Stat check (see margin definition) of STR or DEX. You may announce the use of this ability after seeing the result of the stat check.
Voie de la bravoure|3|desc: The PC makes only a melee attack this turn. In return, they gain an additional attack against any enemy who moves into melee range. An opponent wounded by this attack has their movement stopped.
Voie de la bravoure|4|desc: The PC increases their CON score by +2 and may now roll two d20s whenever a CON check is required and keep the best result.
Voie du combat à deux armes|0|desc:
The PC makes one attack with each hand. They use 1d20 for the attack roll with their main hand and a d12 for the attack with their off-hand. They must wield a light weapon in their off-hand (max 1d6 DMG).
**==Advanced Ability: ==**if they wield two light weapons (max 1d6 DMG each), the PC gains a +1 bonus to attack.
Voie du combat à deux armes|1|desc:
During a full turn, the PC gains a +2 DEF bonus. They make only one attack this turn. If the attack die result is even, the main hand strikes; if odd, the off-hand strikes.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the DEF bonus increases to +4.
Voie du combat à deux armes|2|desc: The PC is no longer required to wield a light weapon in their off-hand. If they wish, they may use a weapon as heavy as their main-hand weapon (d8). If they wield two light weapons (max 1d6 DMG each), they gain a +1 bonus to attack.
Voie du combat à deux armes|3|desc: If both of the PC's weapons hit the target in the same turn, the character gains a combo effect that adds +1d6 DMG to one of the two attacks of their choice.
Voie du combat à deux armes|4|desc: Two-weapon fighting (and consequently cross parry) now only requires an attack action instead of a limited action to use.
Voie du combat monté|0|desc:
The PC owns a powerful warhorse (a warhorse or battle boar). While mounted, they can add a 10 m movement before or after a normal action (e.g. move 10 m and take a limited action). The mount only attacks if it is itself attacked in melee by a creature. 
==**Faithful Mount: **STR +4, DEX +0, CON +4, INT -2, WIS +0, CHA +0, Init. 10, DEF 13, HP 15, kick +5, DMG 1d6+4==
**==Advanced Ability: ==**instead of making an additional movement, the PC may command their mount to attack their target.
Voie du combat monté|1|desc:
While mounted, the PC gains a +2 bonus to melee attack and shares their DEF with their mount — the higher score of the two is kept. Mounting or dismounting is now a free action. 
**==Advanced Ability: ==**while mounted, the PC also gains a +2 bonus to DMG.
Voie du combat monté|2|desc: While mounted, the PC adds +1d6 to DMG against rabble. If at least four creatures with similar statistics are involved in the combat, they are considered rabble (even if their number is later reduced to fewer than four during the fight). Large creatures and mounted riders are never considered rabble.
Voie du combat monté|3|desc: While mounted, the PC can move 40 m in a straight line and make a melee attack at a moment of their choice. They must cover at least 10 m before attacking, but may complete the remaining movement as they see fit. The player rolls two d20s for the attack roll and keeps the best result, while adding +1d6 to DMG. A creature in the path of the charge must succeed at a STR check difficulty 18 or be forced to give way, taking 1d6 DMG. If it succeeds, the charge is blocked and the combatant's turn ends.
Voie du combat monté|4|desc:
The PC gains a flying mount (asp, dragon, wyvern, griffon, or wampür).
==STR +4, DEX +2, CON +4, INT -2, WIS +1, CHA +0, Init. 15, DEF 16, HP [5 × level], attack +8, DMG 1d6+4.== While mounted, the PC may have their mount attack once per turn (free action), on the same turn as the PC, with an attack score equal to their level +3. In flight, the mount covers 30 m per movement action.
Voie du commandement|0|desc:
Once per turn, the PC may swap their initiative with another willing PC. If they give their initiative to a slower PC, they gain a +1 bonus on attack rolls this turn. This bonus increases to +2 at rank 4 of the path.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the attack roll bonus is increased by +1.
Voie du commandement|1|desc: Until their next turn, the PC halves DMG from ranged and area attacks they receive, and may move 20 meters. One of the PC's companions may benefit from this ability if they have the PC's agreement and remain in contact with them. **==Advanced Ability: ==**up to [CHA Mod.] companions (minimum 1) of the PC may benefit from this ability if they have the PC's agreement and remain within 10 meters of them.
Voie du commandement|2|desc: Once per turn, the PC allows an ally fighting the same opponent to reroll the d20 of an attack roll if it was a failure.
Voie du commandement|3|desc: The PC issues sharp tactical orders in the heat of battle. Once per turn, they grant a free additional action to an ally in sight (a movement action or an attack action, but not a limited action).
Voie du commandement|4|desc: Once per combat, on the PC's initiative, the PC and all allies in sight immediately benefit from a movement of up to 20 m in a straight line, followed by an attack action with a +3 bonus to the success roll and +1d6 to damage. Resolve all movements first, starting with the PC, then in initiative order. Then resolve all attacks in the same order.
Voie de la férocité|0|desc:
Once per combat, as an attack action, the PC lets out a roar that frightens their opponents. Any enemy whose STR or maximum HP is lower than the PC's suffers a -2 penalty to their melee attack rolls against the PC for the rest of the combat.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the ability now only requires a movement action.
Voie de la férocité|1|desc:
The PC moves in a straight line at least 5 meters (20 meters maximum) and makes a melee attack with a +2 bonus to attack and +1d6 to DMG. 
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC gains an additional +1 bonus to attack and DMG.
Voie de la férocité|2|desc: The PC enters a berserker rage for the rest of the combat, making them particularly dangerous. They gain a +2 bonus to attack and +1d6 to DMG on all melee attacks, but lose 4 DEF and cannot flee or make ranged attacks. If they wish to end the rage before eliminating all opponents, the PC must succeed at a WIS check difficulty 13 (one attempt per turn).
Voie de la férocité|3|desc: When the PC suffers a critical hit, it only intensifies their rage. They may immediately enter a rage (free action) and gain a +1d6 bonus to DMG on melee attacks for the next three turns. The player notes the additional DMG from the critical hit separately: the PC only loses those HP at the end of the rage.
Voie de la férocité|4|desc: Once per combat, the PC spins around, striking all targets in melee range. They automatically deal DMG equal to their weapon's damage (plus all usual bonuses) to all targets within 5 m of them.
Voie de la guerre|0|desc:
At the start of their turn, the PC may choose to apply up to -1 per rank reached in this path to attack, DEF, or DMG and gain the equivalent as a bonus of their choice to attack, DEF, or DMG until their next turn.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC may apply the bonus to their next turn's initiative instead.
Voie de la guerre|1|desc:
The PC makes a melee attack and the target must make an opposed attack roll. If the PC scores higher, the opponent's weapon falls to the ground (a movement action must be used to pick it up). If the PC's roll exceeds the opponent's by at least 10 points, they prevent the opponent from recovering their weapon (by stepping on it, sending it out of reach, etc.). This ability has no effect on natural weapons (claws, fangs, spine, etc.). Opponents using two-handed weapons are harder to disarm, imposing a -5 penalty to the roll.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the ability may be used as an attack action instead of a limited action.
Voie de la guerre|2|desc: The PC may attempt two melee attacks this turn, each with a -2 penalty.
Voie de la guerre|3|desc: The PC may attempt a melee attack against each opponent engaged in melee with them.
Voie de la guerre|4|desc: The PC may choose to use 1d12 for a melee attack instead of the usual d20 (they still add their normal attack score). If such an attack succeeds, they add +2d6 to DMG. This ability can be used with Double Attack or Sweeping Attack, for example.
Voie de la maîtrise des armes|0|desc:
The PC chooses a weapon of choice (e.g. a longsword or one-handed axe, a short bow, etc.) and gains +1 to attack when using it.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the fighter may choose a second weapon that becomes a familiar weapon. They gain a +1 bonus to attack when using it. The bonus from the Specialization ability does not apply to the familiar weapon.
Voie de la maîtrise des armes|1|desc:
The PC scores criticals on a result of 19 or 20 (18 to 20 when using a rapier). 
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC gains a +1d6 bonus to DMG when scoring a critical hit.
Voie de la maîtrise des armes|2|desc: When the PC uses their weapon of choice, they gain a +2 bonus to DMG.
Voie de la maîtrise des armes|3|desc: Roll two d20s for a melee attack and keep the best result; add +1d6 to DMG.
Voie de la maîtrise des armes|4|desc: In addition to normal actions, once per turn, when an opponent misses a melee attack against the PC, the PC gains an additional attack against that opponent.
Voie du pugilat|0|desc:
When fighting unarmed, the PC may (if they wish) use their ranged attack score instead of their melee attack score. At rank 1 of this path, they deal [1d6 + STR Mod.] lethal DMG. This DMG increases to 1d8 at rank 3 and 1d10 at rank 5.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the DMG die value is increased by one category, up to d12 at rank 5.
Voie du pugilat|1|desc:
The PC is particularly hardened and receives a DEF bonus equal to their CON Mod.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the DEF bonus increases to [CON Mod. +1].
Voie du pugilat|2|desc: The PC no longer feels pain and ignores minor wounds, reducing all DMG taken by 2 points. They still take at least 1 DMG from each attack received.
Voie du pugilat|3|desc: During their turn, the PC may make two unarmed attacks on targets of their choice or two attacks with a weapon. Alternatively, they may choose to make three attacks using a d12 for each attack roll (instead of a d20).
Voie du pugilat|4|desc: The PC increases their STR score by +2 and may now roll two d20s whenever a STR check is required and keep the best result.
Voie de la puissance|0|desc:
The PC adds their STR Mod. to their maximum HP score as well as to their CHA checks and those of adjacent allies for negotiation, persuasion, or intimidation checks. Somehow, their mere presence lends weight to their allies' arguments…
**==Advanced Ability: ==**when rank 4 is reached in this path, the PC adds their STR Mod. to their maximum HP score once more.
Voie de la puissance|1|desc:
The PC can temporarily multiply their physical resources to use prodigious strength. They can lift an incredible load, break a sword, bend bars, or knock down a door with a single punch. They gain a +10 bonus to their STR check, but it costs them 1d4 HP. 
**==Advanced Ability: ==**once per combat, the PC can deliver a blow of incredible violence. They gain a +2d6 DMG bonus but it costs them 2d4 HP.
Voie de la puissance|2|desc: The PC makes a melee attack with a -2 penalty to attack and +1d6 to DMG. At rank 5 in this path, they may choose to take a -5 penalty instead to gain +2d6 to DMG.
Voie de la puissance|3|desc: The PC's critical hits are devastating and cause lasting impairments. The PC scores a critical on 19 or 20 on the d20 for melee attacks. When they score a critical, in addition to doubled DMG, they inflict a -2 penalty on all attack, STR, and DEX checks of the victim. This penalty affects the target until they are completely healed.
Voie de la puissance|4|desc: The PC heals at an almost supernatural rate. As long as they have at least 1 HP remaining, they recover 1d6 HP per hour, day and night.
Voie de l'acrobatie|0|desc:
The PC gains a +5 bonus to all DEX checks aimed at performing acrobatics, maintaining balance, jumping, or climbing.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the bonus increases to +10.
Voie de l'acrobatie|1|desc:
The PC adds their CHA Mod. to DEF and initiative (in addition to the usual DEX Mod.). 
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC adds their CHA Mod. to all stealth and dodge checks.
Voie de l'acrobatie|2|desc: Once per turn, in addition to other actions, the PC may throw a knife or dagger at a ranged target (maximum range: 10 m) by succeeding at a ranged attack roll. This attack is a free action. It deals [1d4 + DEX Mod.] DMG.
Voie de l'acrobatie|3|desc: Once per turn, the PC may perform a dodge by succeeding at a ranged attack roll against a difficulty equal to the score obtained by their opponent during their attack. On success, the PC takes no DMG. If the attack was a critical, they still take normal DMG (thus canceling the critical effect of doubled damage).
Voie de l'acrobatie|4|desc: The PC increases their DEX score by +2 and may now roll two d20s whenever a DEX check is required and keep the best result.
Voie de l'arbalètrie|0|desc:
The PC ignores penalties normally applied when the target is in cover (usually -2 to -5).
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC adds +1 to the weapon's DMG as long as they use it within optimal range, meaning within the weapon's maximum range.
Voie de l'arbalètrie|1|desc:
Reloading a heavy crossbow becomes a movement action. For a hand crossbow or light crossbow, the action becomes free. From rank 5 in this path, the PC can reload two weapons in a single action.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**if their weapon is ready (loaded and in hand), the PC may shoot at their initiative rank +5.
Voie de l'arbalètrie|2|desc: The PC is able to shoot with a hand crossbow in each hand without penalty. If the PC fires both weapons simultaneously at the same target, they make a single roll with a +2 bonus to attack (but roll DMG separately). The player may choose not to fire both shots simultaneously, in order, for example, to target a different enemy if the first is slain. In this case, they receive no attack bonus.
Voie de l'arbalètrie|3|desc: When the PC achieves a DEF of 25 or more on a ranged attack with a crossbow, they add +1d6 to the DMG of that attack.
Voie de l'arbalètrie|4|desc: For a ranged attack, the PC may choose to use a d12 for their attack roll instead of the usual d20 (and they still add their normal attack score). If the attack succeeds, they add +2d6 to DMG. This ability can be used with Double Shot and Crack Shot.
Voie de l'archerie|0|desc:
The PC gains a +5 bonus to all WIS checks involving perception (sight, hearing, alertness, etc.). In addition, they add their WIS Mod. to DMG dealt with a bow. 
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC adds their WIS bonus to initiative.
Voie de l'archerie|1|desc:
The PC may make a ranged attack against an enemy they cannot see (e.g. invisible or hidden in total darkness), but whose existence and approximate position they know, as though they could see them — without the penalty caused by not seeing them. 
**==Advanced Ability: ==**using this ability requires an attack action instead of a limited action.
Voie de l'archerie|2|desc: The PC may make two ranged attacks this turn.
Voie de l'archerie|3|desc: The PC rolls two d20s for their attack and keeps the best result. The arrow's damage is doubled.
Voie de l'archerie|4|desc: For a ranged attack, the PC may choose to use 1d12 for their attack instead of the usual d20 (and they still add their normal ranged attack score). If the attack succeeds, they add 2d6 to DMG. This ability can be used, for example, with Rapid Shot or Death Arrow (the +2d6 DMG bonus is not doubled).
Voie de l'assassinat|0|desc:
When trying to go unnoticed, the PC benefits from a +5 bonus to their DEX check.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the bonus increases to +10.
Voie de l'assassinat|1|desc:
When attacking an opponent from behind* or by surprise, the PC deals 1d6 additional DMG per rank reached in this path (note that sneak attack dice are not multiplied on a critical). 
*==Attacking from behind: when the PC attacks the same creature as an ally, they are considered able to attack it from behind under two conditions: 1. the PC and the ally can position themselves on either side of the target; 2. the target did not attack the PC on its last turn (otherwise, it is considered to be facing the PC).==*
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC may deal a Sneak Attack once per combat against an opponent with a lower initiative than their own using a thrown weapon or thrown dagger.
Voie de l'assassinat|2|desc: Through their mastery of fencing (and trickery), the PC permanently increases their chances of landing critical hits. They now subtract their INT Mod. from the score needed to score a critical on a melee attack. *==For example, Alonzo (INT 14/+2) now scores a critical on 17 or higher on the d20 when using a rapier (19 - INT Mod.).==*
Voie de l'assassinat|3|desc: The PC is never surprised. They may perform a Sneak Attack using an attack action rather than a limited action against a surprised opponent.
Voie de l'assassinat|4|desc: When the PC scores a critical hit on a melee attack, the attack automatically becomes a Sneak Attack. The effects of the Sneak Attack ability apply in addition to the normal effects of the critical.
Voie du bohème|0|desc:
Through years of traveling, the PC has learned all sorts of things. They gain a +5 bonus to INT checks to 'recall' historical, political, geographical, or occult information that might be useful. This bonus can also be applied to identification checks for legendary items.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the bonus increases to +10.
Voie du bohème|1|desc:
The PC can put their logic and wit to work in service of their persuasive power. They gain a +5 bonus to CHA checks aimed at convincing others.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC may also apply this bonus to deceive, lie, or seduce (does not stack with the Charming ability from the Path of Charm).
Voie du bohème|2|desc: The PC gains a +5 bonus to all survival checks in the wild and to profession and crafting checks (blacksmithing, carpentry, etc.).
Voie du bohème|3|desc: With great ingenuity, exceptional acting, and suitable props, the PC can take on the appearance of any creature of roughly equivalent size (within about 50 cm). If subjected to close scrutiny, the adventurer must succeed at an opposed CHA vs. WIS check to avoid being discovered. They need twenty minutes to prepare, and the disguise can last for two hours before the makeup begins to deteriorate.
Voie du bohème|4|desc: The PC may choose any rank 1 to 3 ability of their choice from another fighter or adventurer path. This ability allows upgrading an ability to its advanced form. However, as noted on p. 119, only rank 1 or 2 abilities can be advanced, so a rank 3 ability cannot be taken 'twice'.
Voie du charme|0|desc:
The PC gains a +5 bonus to all CHA tests aimed at seducing, bluffing, or lying.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the bonus rises to +10.
Voie du charme|1|desc:
The PC masters the art of being disagreeable, even insufferable. By winning an opposed CHA vs. INT test, they can force a target to attack them this turn. The PC can immediately retaliate with a free attack.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**if the provoked target misses the PC, the PC can double the DMG of their free attack.
Voie du charme|2|desc: The PC's fighting style is flamboyant and surprising: with this ability, they perform a melee attack with an attack and DMG bonus equal to their CHA Mod. (in addition to the STR or DEX Mod.).
Voie du charme|3|desc: Once per day, the PC can suggest an action to a creature by winning an opposed CHA vs. WIS test. On success, the creature does everything in its power to fulfill the request for 24 hours. It will avoid suicidal actions (which immediately grants it an INT difficulty 10 test to break free). If the suggested action is too incongruous, the target gains a +5 bonus to resist.
Voie du charme|4|desc: The PC increases their CHA value by +2. They can also roll two d20 whenever a CHA test is required and keep the best result.
Voie de la chasse|0|desc:
The PC gains a +5 bonus to survival, stealth, or observation tests in natural environments.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the bonus rises to +10.
Voie de la chasse|1|desc:
If the PC spends an hour in a forest, they find enough food for two people (for one day) per rank reached in this path. By spending 1d6 hours and succeeding a WIS difficulty 10 test, the PC finds medicinal plants to heal 1d6 HP per rank in this path. These plants must be used immediately. The healing can be split among multiple patients: the player decides how many d6 to allocate to each patient.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC can choose another natural environment (plains, mountains, swamp, or desert) and apply the ability's effects. In that environment, they must spend two hours searching for food, and harvested herbs heal only 1d4 HP per rank in this path.
Voie de la chasse|2|desc: The PC can perform a very powerful melee attack. They add their DEX Mod. to attack and DMG for this offensive.
Voie de la chasse|3|desc: After killing a creature, the PC can decide that all members of its race have become sworn enemies. Against these creatures, they gain a bonus equal to their WIS Mod. on attacks and inflict +1d6 DMG. At level 8, they can choose a second sworn enemy. The PC can change their sworn enemy once per level.
Voie de la chasse|4|desc: The PC increases their WIS value by +2 and can now roll two d20 whenever a WIS test is required and keep the best result.
Voie du compagnon animal|0|desc:
The PC's wolf detects the scents of animals and creatures where they have passed. The PC gains a +5 bonus to WIS tests to track and follow trails.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**The wolf can help the PC detect illusions and false appearances. It adds its bonus to tests not to be fooled by these tricks.
Voie du compagnon animal|1|desc:
The wolf is constantly on guard. The PC therefore gets advance warning when enemies try to ambush them, gaining a +5 bonus to surprise tests (WIS) and initiative. When not surprised, the PC can make a simple ranged attack before the first combat turn begins.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC can no longer be surprised.
Voie du compagnon animal|2|desc:
The PC's wolf can now fight like a true character. It attacks at the same time as the PC.
==**Wolf: **Init. 13, DEF 14, HP [level × 4], melee attack = [PC's level], DMG 1d6+1, STR +1, DEX +1, CON* +1, INT -3, WIS* +2, CHA -2.==
Voie du compagnon animal|3|desc: The PC can speak with animals. Additionally, when spending a Recovery Point to recover HP, their wolf also regains HP equal to [1d8 + wolf's CON Mod. + PC's level].
Voie du compagnon animal|4|desc:
The PC's wolf becomes a particularly powerful specimen.
==**Alpha Male: **Init. 13, DEF 16, HP [PC's level × 4], melee attack = [PC's level + 2], DMG 1d6+3, STR +3, DEX +1, CON* +3, INT -3, WIS* +2, CHA -2==
Voie de l'escrime|0|desc:
The PC can use their ranged attack score for a melee attack when using a dueling weapon or a dagger.
**==Advanced Ability:==** the PC can apply the DEF bonus from an off-hand weapon (see "Equipment" page 156) against ranged attacks.
Voie de l'escrime|1|desc:
The PC adds their INT Mod. to initiative and DEF in addition to their DEX Mod.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**if desired, the PC can replace the STR Mod. on DMG with their INT Mod.
Voie de l'escrime|2|desc: The PC performs a fake attack to unbalance their opponent and then delivers a deadly blow. Make a normal attack test this turn but inflict no DMG. Next turn, if attacking the same opponent, gain a +5 bonus to attack and, if the feint succeeded, +2d6 to DMG. At rank 5 in this path, the attack bonus from the feint rises to +10.
Voie de l'escrime|3|desc: The PC can now use a weapon in each hand without penalty when using dueling weapons or daggers. With their off-hand, they can make a free additional melee attack each turn. This second attack does not prevent the use of a limited action, but it cannot be the origin of one.
Voie de l'escrime|4|desc: During a melee attack, if the total attack score is 10 or more points above the opponent's DEF, the PC gains a +2d6 DMG bonus.
Voie de la fourberie|0|desc:
The PC gains a +5 bonus to all DEX tests related to precision: picking a lock, disarming a trap, picking someone's pocket…
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the bonus rises to +10.
Voie de la fourberie|1|desc:
By succeeding an INT difficulty 10 test, the PC can detect (and safely bypass) traps before they trigger. Magical traps require an INT difficulty 15 test.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**detection of non-magical traps succeeds automatically.
Voie de la fourberie|2|desc: When getting a score of 17 to 20 on a melee attack d20, the PC trips their opponent in addition to inflicting normal DMG. A score of 19 or 20 is required against a four-legged creature.
Voie de la fourberie|3|desc: Once per combat, the PC can, by succeeding a melee attack, paralyze an opponent with pain. The target takes no DMG but cannot attack or move for 1d4 turns.
Voie de la fourberie|4|desc: Once per turn, when an ally successfully wounds a creature in melee contact with the PC, the PC can make a free normal attack against it, taking advantage of the opening.
Voie de l'alchimie|0|desc:
A strange brew that heals [1d4 + rank] HP and grants a +3 bonus to the next [rank + 1] tests made (within 12 hours). Drinking a restorative requires a move action.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC has two additional restoratives beyond the elixirs they can normally prepare each day.
Voie de l'alchimie|1|desc:
The PC throws the vial up to 10 m away using an attack action (automatic hit). The contents explode in a 3 m radius inflicting 1d6 DMG per rank in this path. A DEX difficulty [10 + INT Mod.] test allows victims to halve DMG.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the Greek fire flames persist for one turn, inflicting 1 fire DMG per rank in this path to any target starting its turn in or crossing the area. Succeeding a DEX difficulty [10 + INT Mod.] test cancels the DMG.
Voie de l'alchimie|2|desc: Once exposed to air, the oil ignites spontaneously and burns intensely. As a limited action, it can be applied to a weapon to gain +1d6 fire DMG for [5 + INT Mod.] minutes. The oil can also be applied to crossbow bolts or arrowheads using one attack action per batch of projectiles; one dose ignites about five projectiles.
Voie de l'alchimie|3|desc: The PC can prepare an elixir that heals [3d6 + INT Mod.] HP, a poisoning, or a hemorrhage.
Voie de l'alchimie|4|desc: The PC can prepare a Bark Skin potion (rank 1 of the Forest Path), a Blur potion (rank 1 of the Illusion Path), or a Protection from Elements potion (rank 3 of the Elementalist Magic Path). The effects of these potions are not cumulative.
Voie des arts druidiques|0|desc:
The PC can communicate with animals, who generally behave in a friendly manner toward them. The PC gains a +5 bonus to all tests aimed at influencing an animal. Communication remains primitive and limited to the animal's intelligence and point of view (predator, prey, etc.).
**==Advanced Ability: ==**by succeeding an INT difficulty 20 test (the ability bonus applies), the PC can hold a much more in-depth conversation with the animal, lending it humanoid reasoning.
Voie des arts druidiques|1|desc:
By succeeding a magic attack test (range 20 m), the PC releases a swarm of flying insects onto a target that sting, blind, and follow it for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns. The victim takes 1 DMG per turn and suffers a -2 penalty to all actions. Area DMG destroys the swarm.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the victim takes 2 DMG per turn and a -3 penalty to all actions.
Voie des arts druidiques|2|desc:
The PC receives a bird of prey as an animal companion. The PC has a telepathic link with it and can perceive through its senses with a +5 bonus to perception-based tests.
**Bird of prey: **Init. 15, DEF 15, HP [PC's level × 3]. Melee attack = [PC's magic attack], DMG 1d4.
Voie des arts druidiques|3|desc: While under this spell, the PC takes on the features of a great cat or wolf. The PC gains their INT Mod. to initiative, attack, and DMG and can see in the dark (like an Elf) for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns.
Voie des arts druidiques|4|desc: The PC increases their WIS score by +2 and can now roll two d20 whenever a WIS test is required and keep the best result.
Voie de la divination|0|desc:
The PC always knows slightly in advance what is about to happen. They gain a +2 bonus to initiative and DEF, as well as a +5 bonus to all tests to avoid being surprised.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the initiative and DEF bonus rises to +3. The bonus to avoid being surprised rises to +10.
Voie de la divination|1|desc:
For [5 + WIS Mod.] turns, the PC detects invisible or hidden creatures within 30 meters and perceives if a Clairvoyance spell affects the area. Blinded or in total darkness, this spell allows the PC to see normally.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC can cast this spell as a move action instead of an attack action, but cannot use concentration in those conditions.
Voie de la divination|2|desc: The PC can see and hear at a distance what is happening in a place they know, as long as they remain concentrated (limited action each turn). Creatures present have a right to a WIS difficulty [12 + INT Mod.] test: on success, they feel observed.
Voie de la divination|3|desc: Once per combat, at the end of a turn, the PC can decide that everything that happened during that turn was merely a vision of a possible future. The turn is replayed from the beginning: other PCs, creatures, and NPCs cannot change their actions, unlike the PC, who benefits from a +10 bonus to initiative and DEF.
Voie de la divination|4|desc: The PC increases their WIS and INT scores by +2. They can now roll two d20 whenever a WIS or INT test is required and keep the best result.
Voie de l'envoûtement|0|desc:
The PC gives a target a simple (but not dangerous to it) order that the target must be able to understand. The PC must succeed at a magic attack test opposed against the target (if the creature has no magic attack, use its melee attack with a -3 penalty) at a range of 20 meters. On failure, the target executes the order on its next turn.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC gains a +2 bonus to their magic attack test.
Voie de l'envoûtement|1|desc:
[1d6 + INT Mod.] living targets in a 10 m diameter area (range: 20 m) whose current HP score does not exceed the PC's magic attack score fall unconscious for [5 + WIS Mod.] minutes. They can be awakened by slapping (attack action). *Creatures with HP between the PC's magic attack score and double that may make a WIS difficulty [10 + INT Mod.] test to resist. Creatures with higher HP are unaffected.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the WIS test difficulty to resist the spell rises to [12 + INT Mod.].
Voie de l'envoûtement|2|desc: By winning an opposed magic attack test (range: 20 m), the PC disorients the target for [3 + WIS Mod.] turns. Each turn, roll 1d6: on 1–3, the victim does not act; on 4–6, it attacks the nearest creature. At the end of each turn, it makes a WIS difficulty [10 + INT Mod.] test to end the spell.
Voie de l'envoûtement|3|desc: If the PC succeeds a magic attack test (range: 10 m) against a humanoid target's maximum HP score, that creature behaves as a longtime friend as long as it is not attacked. It can resist the spell with a WIS difficulty [12 + INT Mod.] test, renewable once per day.
Voie de l'envoûtement|4|desc: By winning an opposed magic attack test (range: 20 m) against the target's magic attack (if the creature has no magic attack, use its melee attack with a -3 penalty), the PC takes control of its mind for [1d4 + WIS Mod.] minutes. The PC's own body becomes inactive. The victim can resist with a WIS difficulty [10 + INT Mod.] test each time it takes DMG. If the creature dies during the domination, the PC returns to their body and takes 1d6 DMG.
Voie des forêts|0|desc:
The PC's skin takes on the texture of bark. They gain +2 DEF for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns. This bonus rises to +4 at rank 4 of this path.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC applies the DEF bonus to all CON tests to resist poisons.
Voie des forêts|1|desc:
The PC can command vegetation to grow and block enemies (but not allies) in a 10 m diameter area (range: 20 m) for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns. Restrained targets suffer a -2 penalty to attack and DEF and cannot move. Each turn, a creature can break free with a STR difficulty [10 + INT Mod.] test.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**restrained targets suffer a -3 penalty to attack and DEF.
Voie des forêts|2|desc: The PC must be in front of a living bush or tree. Their incantation causes [1d6 + INT Mod.] fruits to grow and be harvested. Each fruit provides the equivalent of a meal and restores [1d10 + PC's level] HP to whoever consumes it. The effects of these fruits only work once per day per character.
Voie des forêts|3|desc:
Once per combat, the PC can animate a tree by touching it. It fights for [PC's level] turns.
==**Animated Tree: **Init. 7, DEF 13, HP [rank × 10], melee attack = [PC's level], DMG 1d6+3, movement 10 m per move action. DMG reduction of 10, except against slashing weapons and fire.==
Voie des forêts|4|desc: The target touched by the PC heals 3 HP per turn for [PC's level + WIS Mod.] turns. This spell cannot affect a creature more than once per day.
Voie des illusions|0|desc:
For [5 + WIS Mod.] turns, when an attack hits the PC, roll 1d6: on 5–6, the PC takes no DMG. Area DMG applies normally.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC can apply the spell's effects to area DMG and effects.
Voie des illusions|1|desc:
The PC creates a stationary visual and auditory illusion lasting [5 + WIS Mod.] minutes (or turns if the illusion is animated). The maximum volume of the illusion is 10 m per side per rank in this path (range: 500 m). Divide these parameters by 10 if the illusion is animated. Interacting with the illusion makes it disappear.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the spell duration rises to [30 + WIS Mod.] minutes.
Voie des illusions|2|desc: The PC can take on the appearance of a similarly-sized creature (within +/– 50 cm) that they see at the time of casting. Duration: [5 + WIS Mod.] minutes. Touching the PC (by attack or otherwise) ends the spell.
Voie des illusions|3|desc: On a successful magic attack (range: 20 m), the PC creates a translucent double of the target for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns. The double is under the PC's control. It has the same stats as the original but only half its HP, and all DMG it inflicts are halved. It disappears if its HP reaches 0. A creature can only be duplicated once per combat.
Voie des illusions|4|desc: This spell invokes the worst fears of a living creature and makes it believe it is dying. The PC must succeed an opposed magic attack test (range: 20 m). The victim makes a WIS difficulty [10 + INT Mod.] test to resist. On failure, it falls to 0 HP. On success, it loses its balance and falls. A creature can only be targeted by this spell once per day, and the PC cannot affect a character or creature of a higher level than their own.
Voie de la magie élémentaliste|0|desc:
The PC creates a very dense fog up to 20 m range over a 20 m diameter area. Visibility in this area is greatly reduced, equivalent to dim light. It is not possible to see beyond the fog, which persists for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**by doubling the MP cost, the PC can double the area or the duration of the spell — while applying concentration effects if desired.
Voie de la magie élémentaliste|1|desc:
The PC becomes charged with electrical energy for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns. Any creature that wounds them in melee or touches them receives a discharge inflicting 1d6 DMG. The PC can also deliver an electrical discharge (magic attack, range: 10 m) inflicting [1d6 + INT Mod.] DMG. At rank 4 of this path, DMG rises to 2d6.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**by doubling the MP cost, the PC can cast the spell immediately when attacked. The PC must not be surprised and cannot use concentration.
Voie de la magie élémentaliste|2|desc: For [5 + WIS Mod.] turns, the PC subtracts from all fire, cold, electricity, or acid DMG received an amount equal to twice their rank in this path.
Voie de la magie élémentaliste|3|desc: The PC chooses a target within 30 meters. They make a magic attack test and compare it to the DEF of all characters (including the PC and companions) within a 6 m radius of the target. Each target hit takes [4d6 + INT Mod.] DMG. On failure, the target takes only half DMG.
Voie de la magie élémentaliste|4|desc: The PC increases their INT value by +2. They can now roll two d20 whenever an INT test is required and keep the best result.
Voie de la magie runique|0|desc:
The PC gains a +5 bonus to goldsmithing or forging tests. As a move action, they can ignite their weapon for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns, gaining a DMG bonus of +1 per rank in this path (fire DMG).
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the bonus to tests rises to +10, and igniting their weapon requires only a free action instead of a move action.
Voie de la magie runique|1|desc:
By performing a five-minute ritual, the PC enchants a piece of jewelry for 24 hours. It allows re-rolling a d20 once per combat on an attack, STR, DEX, or CON test. Only one such piece of jewelry can be worn.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**each day, the first energy rune created by the PC costs no MP.
Voie de la magie runique|2|desc: By performing a five-minute ritual, the PC enchants a weapon for 24 hours. It allows dealing maximum DMG, without rolling the die, once per combat.
Voie de la magie runique|3|desc:
The PC inscribes a luminous rune in the air (visible for the duration of the spell) and traps an area from 2 to 10 m in diameter. Any creature (at least very small size) that enters the area triggers one of three effects chosen when the spell is cast.
**==• Alarm: ==**a powerful gong sounds.
**==• Fire: ==**[3d6 + INT Mod.] fire DMG.
**==• Fear: ==**WIS difficulty [15 + INT Mod.] test or flee for 2d6 turns.
Each time a creature enters the area again, it suffers the effects. The spell lasts 1d6 minutes but can be extended up to 12 hours with a 10-minute ritual. Creatures present during the ritual are immune to the effects. Additionally, if this spell is cast while another rune is still active, the old one ends immediately.
Voie de la magie runique|4|desc: The PC can enchant objects. They can create a weapon or armor whose magic level equals a maximum of their [level / 3] rounded up. The PC can enchant the object with the traditional enchantments of their people (exception: Humans must choose a tradition from among those of the Elves, Dwarves, Ogres, Orcs, and Goblins). From level 8, the PC can also use legendary enchantments. Everything must be agreed upon with the GM. More information in the "Traditional and Legendary Objects" chapter of the *==Game Master's Book==*. It takes one month of work and 100 gp per magic level to create the object. The PC must infuse a little vital energy into the object: they sacrifice 1 HP permanently from their maximum HP score.
Voie du mysticisme|0|desc:
The PC chants a song to encourage their companions in sight. These companions (and the PC) gain a +1 bonus to all Stat. and attack tests for [3 + WIS Mod.] turns. The bonus rises to +2 at rank 5 in this path.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the bonus to tests is increased by 1.
Voie du mysticisme|1|desc:
The PC can cast this spell on themselves or any ally (range: contact) for the duration of a combat. The target gains a +2 bonus to DEF and to all resistance tests against attacks from undead, demons, elementals, and conjured creatures (summoned from another plane by magic).
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the DEF bonus rises to +4.
Voie du mysticisme|2|desc: By touching the target, the PC cancels penalties inflicted by spells, curses, petrification, and special abilities of other characters or creatures (pain, mutilation, poisons, etc.).
Voie du mysticisme|3|desc: When the PC casts this spell, all opponents wishing to attack them must first succeed a WIS difficulty 15 test. If they fail, they cannot attack the PC. The effects last [5 + WIS Mod.] turns, but if the PC attempts any attack, the spell ends immediately.
Voie du mysticisme|4|desc: On a magic attack, the PC can use a d12 instead of the usual d20. If the attack succeeds, they add 2d6 to DMG. This ability is only used with spells requiring an attack test and inflicting DMG. If DMG are spread over several turns, only the initial DMG are modified.
Voie des sombres savoirs|0|desc:
The PC must succeed a magic attack test (range: 10 m). Blood flows from the mouth, nose, ears, and even eyes of the target, who takes 1d6 DMG per turn for [rank in path] turns. This spell ignores a target's DR, but creatures that do not bleed are immune (such as non-living creatures, ghouls, or dryads). DMG rises to 1d8 at rank 4 in this path.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the spell duration becomes [rank in path + WIS Mod.] turns.
Voie des sombres savoirs|1|desc:
The PC makes a magic attack test against a target within 20 meters. On success, the victim makes all tests with two d20 and keeps the lowest result. The spell ends once it has failed three tests (no time limit). If the target has an ability allowing it to roll two dice and keep the best, it now rolls only one die. Only one target at a time can be affected by a curse.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**the PC can affect a total of [INT Mod.] targets (minimum 1). The spell must be cast for each target.
Voie des sombres savoirs|2|desc: As a free action, the PC sacrifices 1d4 HP and immediately gains +3 on a d20 roll of their choice or to DEF against an attack. From rank 5 in this path, they can sacrifice 2d4 HP to raise this bonus to +5.
Voie des sombres savoirs|3|desc: If the PC succeeds a magic attack test (range: 10 m), the victim bleeds at the slightest wound. All bleeding attack DMG inflicted on the target (bladed weapon, claw, bleeding, etc.) increase by 1d6 for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns. This spell ignores a target's DR, but creatures that do not bleed are immune (such as non-living creatures, ghouls, or dryads).
Voie des sombres savoirs|4|desc:
Once per combat, by sacrificing 1d6 HP, the PC summons a demon that enters their service for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns. The demon is a large black silhouette about 2.30 m tall, with blurry indeterminate contours and wide scarlet eyes. The demon halves all non-magical DMG it suffers; spells and magical weapons inflict normal DMG. It can fly at a speed equivalent to a normal movement. When the PC reaches level 10, the demon can attack twice per turn using a limited action.
==**Demon: **STR +5*, DEX +2, CON +4*, INT +2, WIS +2, CHA +0, Init. 16, DEF 17, HP [PC's level × 5], melee attack = [PC's level], DMG 1d8+5==
Voie des armes lourdes|0|desc: The PC gains a +1 bonus to attack and +5 to Init. when using a two-handed weapon.
Voie des armes lourdes|1|desc: For a limited action, the PC makes an attack against a target. It takes +1d6 DMG in addition to the weapon's normal DMG and must succeed on an opposed STR test or be knocked down.
Voie des armes lourdes|2|desc: During this turn, the PC makes an attack action and uses its reach to keep opponents at a distance. It gains a +5 bonus to DEF against melee attacks.
Voie des armes lourdes|3|desc: The PC may choose a -2 DEF penalty to gain a +1d6 DMG bonus, or a -5 DEF penalty to gain a +2d6 DMG bonus. This bonus is not multiplied on a critical hit.
Voie des armes lourdes|4|desc: When scoring a critical hit with a two-handed weapon, the PC gains +2d6 DMG in addition to the critical's normal effects.
Voie de la danse de guerre|0|desc: The PC's dance steps make it elusive, granting +2 to DEF. Additionally, it gains a +5 bonus to all dance and acrobatics tests.
Voie de la danse de guerre|1|desc: The PC may use its DEX Mod. either in melee attack or in DMG (but not both) instead of STR Mod. when wielding a war weapon or a polearm.
Voie de la danse de guerre|2|desc: Each time the PC uses a limited action, it may also move 10 meters before or after.
Voie de la danse de guerre|3|desc: At the cost of a limited action, the PC enters a trance for the rest of the combat. During the Blade Dance, it gains +2 to attack and DEF and may make one extra free attack using a d12 instead of the d20 each turn. The character may end the trance at will but cannot start it again until the next combat. However, if it takes DMG from a critical hit, the trance ends immediately.
Voie de la danse de guerre|4|desc: Each turn the PC attacks a different target than the previous turn, it gains a +2 bonus to attack and +1d6 to DMG.
Voie du dogme de pierre|0|desc: The PC can no longer be knocked down or pushed back against its will and seems to weigh several tons! Additionally, it gains +1 to DEF, increasing to +2 at rank 3 in this path.
Voie du dogme de pierre|1|desc: The PC senses the presence of all creatures in contact with stone (floor or walls) within a 20-meter radius. It can precisely determine their position, even in darkness or if they are invisible. It can no longer be approached by surprise by any opponent in contact with the ground.
Voie du dogme de pierre|2|desc: The PC's blows carry the weight and impact of rock. When it rolls a 1 on a DMG die for a melee attack, that result is replaced by the maximum value of the die used (for example, 6 on a d6).
Voie du dogme de pierre|3|desc: On a successful unarmed melee attack, the PC petrifies its target. The target must succeed on a CON test with difficulty [10 + rank in this path] or be unable to act for 1d6 minutes. While petrified, its body becomes extremely hard and it subtracts 5 points from all DMG received. If the target loses more than 5 HP while petrified, the effect ends. A creature can only be targeted by this ability once per combat.
Voie du dogme de pierre|4|desc: The PC gives its body the texture of stone for [1d6 + WIS Mod.] turns. In this state, it subtracts 5 points from all DMG dealt by weapons. Creatures that attack it with natural weapons take 1 DMG per attack, and those using manufactured weapons may find their weapon shattered if they roll a 1 on their DMG die. Traditional weapons are not at risk of shattering.
Voie de l'ordre ailé|0|desc: The PC joins a prestigious chivalric order with the rank of apprentice. It receives a flying mount, insignia, and a one-handed weapon set with the symbols of its order. It acquires rank 5 of the Path of Mounted Combat. If it already has this ability or acquires it later, it becomes a full ranked member of its order and gains a +5 bonus to all persuasion and intimidation tests. This bonus increases to +10 while riding its flying mount.
Voie de l'ordre ailé|1|desc: Depending on its order and the nature of its mount, the PC can imbue its weapon with an elemental aura for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns, dealing an extra +1d6 elemental DMG.
Voie de l'ordre ailé|2|desc: The bond between the PC and its mount grows stronger. It subtracts 5 points from all DMG of its element (e.g., fire DR for dragons, lightning DR for griffins, etc.). This reduction increases to 10 at rank 5 in this path.
Voie de l'ordre ailé|3|desc:
The PC's mount reaches full maturity and increases its offensive capabilities: 
==STR +7, DEX +2, CON +7, INT -2, WIS +1, CHA +0, Init. 15, DEF 18, HP 20 + [5 × level], attack +12, DMG 2d6+7.==
Voie de l'ordre ailé|4|desc: The PC's mount can now use an elemental breath as its attack action. If it succeeds on an attack test, it deals 4d6 elemental DMG to its target and 2d6 elemental DMG to all targets within 3 meters of the initial target. On a failure, all targets take only 1d6 DMG. This breath has a range of 40 m. The element type depends on the mount (fire for dragons, ice for wyverns, etc.).
Voie de la purification|0|desc: The PC concentrates on a creature within 30 meters. It detects whether that creature possesses paranormal powers (mystic path or magical abilities) if it succeeds on a WIS test with difficulty 15. Humanoid creatures have the right to an opposed INT test to resist this divination. Only one attempt per creature is possible.
Voie de la purification|1|desc: The PC gains a +1 bonus to attack and DMG against undead, demons, and corrupted animals (those with paranormal powers). This bonus increases to +2 at rank 4 in the path.
Voie de la purification|2|desc: The PC gains +2 to DEF against all magical attacks and +2 to DMG against mystics.
Voie de la purification|3|desc: In addition to dealing normal DMG, this attack forces the target to make an INT test with difficulty 15. On a failure, it cannot use any magical or extraordinary ability on its next turn.
Voie de la purification|4|desc: Once per combat, the PC completely resists a spell or magical effect of its choice. Additionally, it is immune to corruption effects: drain, weakening, rot, poisoning, or disease caused by undead, demons, or malevolent or corrupted animals.
Voie de la rage ancestrale|0|desc: The PC gains a +5 bonus to all intimidation tests and acquires rank 1 of the Path of Ferocity. If it already has this ability or acquires it later, it may add this bonus to its STR when estimating the enemies affected by the war cry, and the action required to use it is reduced by one category. Thus, War Cry requires only a movement action while the advanced War Cry becomes a free action.
Voie de la rage ancestrale|1|desc: The PC acquires rank 3 of the Path of Ferocity. If it already has this ability or acquires it later, it transforms into Berserker Fury, granting +3 to attack and +2d6 to DMG for a -6 DEF penalty. The WIS test difficulty to end the rage early increases to 16.
Voie de la rage ancestrale|2|desc: Once per combat, the character may grab an opponent of equal or lesser size and crush it in its powerful arms. On a successful melee attack, the PC deals [2d6 + STR Mod.] DMG to its target (+ any rage bonus) and immobilizes it, imposing a -5 DEF penalty on the opponent. On its turn, the target may attempt to break free with an opposed STR test; on a failure, it can take no action. Each subsequent turn while the hold is maintained, the PC automatically deals DMG again without needing an attack test.
Voie de la rage ancestrale|3|desc: The PC can maintain full clarity while entering a rage or fury. It suffers no DEF penalty and may use throwing weapons. However, it cannot use other ranged weapons (bow or crossbow) or attempt to negotiate while in this state.
Voie de la rage ancestrale|4|desc: When under the effects of rage or fury for at least three turns, the player may choose to double the DMG* of one attack, which immediately ends the rage. The character cannot re-enter rage during the combat. *Double all DMG (including rage bonuses). The player must declare the use of this ability before rolling the DMG dice, but after seeing the attack test result.
Voie du sang draconique|0|desc: Thanks to the dragon blood that flows in its veins, the PC gains a 5-point reduction on fire DMG. This resistance increases to 10 at rank 4 in this path.
Voie du sang draconique|1|desc: The PC's muscles develop spectacularly for [1d6 + WIS Mod.] turns while enormous claws suddenly grow from its fingers. Its STR score rises to 18 (or gains a +2 bonus if it is already equal to or greater than that value) and it gains a claw melee attack dealing [1d6 + STR Mod.] DMG.
Voie du sang draconique|2|desc: Once per combat, as an attack action, the PC can produce a lethal fire breath. This breath covers a conical area with a range of 5 meters and a width of 5 meters at its far end, dealing 2d6 DMG per rank achieved in this path. Targets within the cone may halve the DMG by succeeding on a DEX test with difficulty [10 + rank].
Voie du sang draconique|3|desc: The PC's body strengthens and becomes covered in scales when it receives serious wounds. When the character drops below half of its HP, it gains a 5-point damage reduction against all types of DMG.
Voie du sang draconique|4|desc: Once per day, the PC can unfurl dragon wings for [1d6 + WIS Mod.] minutes. These wings allow it to fly at a speed of 30 meters per movement action. Hovering is a free action. Each new use of this ability requires spending 1 RP beforehand.
Voie de l'archerie arcanique|0|desc: The PC enchants its arrows. If it rolls a 1 on its DMG die, it rerolls the die and keeps the second result. The DMG dealt by its arrows are considered magical.
Voie de l'archerie arcanique|1|desc: The PC crafts and enchants a special arrow for a unique named enemy. It requires a full day to create the arrow and may possess only one at a time (neither for the same target nor for another). When using this arrow against the enemy it was made for, the PC gains a +3 bonus to attack and +1d6 to DMG per rank achieved in the path. If the arrow was created more than a year ago, these bonuses are doubled.
Voie de l'archerie arcanique|2|desc: The PC can use arrows capable of animating vegetation. Once per combat, as an attack action, it may cast the Plant Prison spell (Path of Forests, rank 2) in its advanced version at range up to the bow's maximum without spending MP.
Voie de l'archerie arcanique|3|desc: The adventurer enchants its arrows to give them life upon contact with blood. Embedded in a body, they burrow in and take root. On a result of 15 to 20 on the attack test (or 10 to 12 on a d12), the arrow deals an additional +2d6 DMG and transforms into a shrub upon the victim's death.
Voie de l'archerie arcanique|4|desc: The PC enchants its arrow to allow it to pass through physical obstacles. The player makes a normal attack test but replaces DEF with the target's DEX score as the Difficulty. Furthermore, it ignores all cover penalties. If it knows precisely where its target is, it may even fire through a wall or door.
Voie de l'audace|0|desc: When taking an action in a dangerous location (for example, at the edge of a precipice or a lava lake), the PC gains a +3 bonus to all d20 tests (attack, Stat tests, etc.). This bonus also applies to tests made to resist fear.
Voie de l'audace|1|desc: The PC may choose to sacrifice a movement action on this turn to gain +3 to DEF until its next turn. If it sacrifices two movement actions, it gains +6 to DEF.
Voie de l'audace|2|desc: When the result of a stat test could be fatal for the PC (fatal fall, awakening a dragon, etc.), the player rolls an extra d20 and may keep whichever result they prefer.
Voie de l'audace|3|desc: By spending 1d4 HP, the PC gains an extra movement action on its turn. Combined with Gadfly, the PC can thus sacrifice three movement actions and gain a +10 bonus to DEF!
Voie de l'audace|4|desc: The PC literally leaps onto its target to pummel it in close combat. Rather than an attack test, it makes a DEX test with a difficulty equal to half the creature's DEF. On a success, it deals normal DMG and receives a bonus based on the creature's size: medium +1d6 DMG, large +2d6, very large +3d6, huge +4d6, and colossal +5d6. It receives no DMG bonus against small creatures. Following this attack, it loses 5 points of DEF for 1 turn.
Voie du duel|0|desc: The PC may use its DEX Mod. either in melee attack or in DMG (but not both) instead of STR Mod. when wielding a dueling weapon or a sword.
Voie du duel|1|desc: Once per combat, the PC may challenge a target of its choice (range: 30 meters). It gains a +2 bonus to attack and DEF for the rest of the combat against that target.
Voie du duel|2|desc: On each turn that it attacks the challenged target, the PC gains a +1 DEF bonus per rank achieved in the path against all attacks from other opponents.
Voie du duel|3|desc: Once per turn, the PC may attempt to parry a melee attack from the opponent it has challenged. It must succeed on a melee attack test at least equal to the one obtained by its opponent to parry or dodge the chosen attack.
Voie du duel|4|desc: Once per turn, the PC may make an opposed INT test against an opponent in melee range. If it wins, it may reroll one attack or DMG die against that opponent before the end of the turn.
Voie de la flibusterie|0|desc: The PC gains a +5 bonus on all DEX tests made on a ship or other moving surfaces (including carts, ropes, rope bridges). It also gains a +5 bonus to all swimming and navigation tests.
Voie de la flibusterie|1|desc: Once per turn, at a moment of its choosing, the PC may make a free attack with the stock of an unloaded crossbow. It uses a d12 for the attack test and deals [1d4 + STR Mod.] DMG.
Voie de la flibusterie|2|desc: The PC knows how to launch itself at enemies and put heart into its first melee attack in a combat. On this initial attack, it gains a +5 bonus to attack and +2d6 to DMG.
Voie de la flibusterie|3|desc: The PC may fire a hand crossbow with one hand and make a melee attack without penalty with a second weapon held in its other hand.
Voie de la flibusterie|4|desc: For the PC, it is conquer or die. Its ferocity is legendary: it may attempt a free attack action against any creature at its melee range that uses a movement action to move away from it. For this attack, it gains a +5 bonus to attack and +1d6 to DMG.
Voie de la lycanthropie|0|desc:
At the cost of a limited action, the PC may transform into a hybrid form, half-humanoid half-wolf or half-panther, for [5 × WIS Mod.] turns. In this form, it can no longer use weapons, but may still use its armor, which adapts to its size. It gains the following Stat bonuses and attacks: 
==**Wolf Lycanthropy**
STR +2 - claws [melee attack], DMG [2d6 + STR Mod.] and bite [melee attack], DMG [1d6 + STR Mod.]. Only an attack action is needed to use both attack modes in the same turn. 
**Feline Lycanthropy**
DEX +2 - claws [melee or ranged attack], DMG [2d4 + STR or DEX Mod.] and bite [melee attack], DMG [1d4 + STR or DEX Mod.]. Only an attack action is needed to use both attack modes in the same turn.==
Voie de la lycanthropie|1|desc: If the PC succeeds in hitting and wounding with both attacks, it eviscerates its target and gains a +2d6 DMG bonus on its second attack.
Voie de la lycanthropie|2|desc:
The PC may take the form of a wolf or panther for a maximum of 1 hour per character level per day. The character retains all stats except those below, as well as its normal melee attack score. 
==**Wolf:** Init. 17, DEF 15, DMG 1d6+3. STR +3, DEX +1. +5 bonus to all perception-based tests. 
**Panther:** Init. 23, DEF 16, DMG 1d6+2. STR +2, DEX +4*.==
In this form, it can use the ability Pouncing Attack (L): the PC covers up to 30 m and gains a +5 bonus to the attack test and +2d6 to DMG against its target. It must travel at least 5 m in a straight line to use this attack and may only do so once per opponent. Shifting from animal form to hybrid form requires only a movement action.
Voie de la lycanthropie|3|desc: The PC gains a +1 bonus to attack and DMG for every 10 HP lost during the combat against the target that dealt those DMG (maximum +5). These bonuses last until the end of combat and are cancelled if the target dies.
Voie de la lycanthropie|4|desc: The lycanthrope's fur hardens while its bones solidify to become as strong as steel. The PC gains a +3 bonus to DEF and a DR of 3 while transformed as a lycanthrope (animal or hybrid form).
Voie des poisons|0|desc: The PC may make an INT test with difficulty 10 to recognize, identify, or detect a poison. It no longer needs an INT test to successfully apply a poison to a weapon.
Voie des poisons|1|desc: The PC knows how to prepare small quantities of 'fast-acting' poison. Before each combat, its weapons (maximum three) are coated with this poison. The first successful attack deals an additional 2d6 DMG. If the target succeeds on a CON test with difficulty [10 + rank in this path], it only takes half the poison DMG. The fast-acting poison's DMG increases to 3d6 at rank 4 of the path.
Voie des poisons|2|desc: The PC may replace the fast-acting poison with a weakening poison: its effects are only applied on the first successful hit with that weapon. If the target fails a CON test with difficulty 15, it is weakened for the rest of the combat (see 'Afflictions').
Voie des poisons|3|desc: From years of handling poisons, the PC has developed immunities. When poisoned, make a CON test with difficulty 10. On a success, it suffers no effects; on a failure, only half.
Voie des poisons|4|desc: The PC knows how to produce small quantities of 'slow' and 'violent' poisons and may use [1 + INT Mod.] doses per day (combined count of both poison types). The resistance difficulty for these poisons is [12 + INT Mod.].
Voie des rituels|0|desc: To perform this ritual, a humanoid corpse dead for no more than [rank × 4] days is required — the more advanced the decomposition, the less intelligible the subsequent exchange. For one hour, it is possible to speak with what remains of the departed's soul and ask it any questions the PC wishes, to which the dead will answer in a disembodied, emotionless voice. The dead will state facts according to their own perspective and biases at the time of death, which may sometimes introduce distortion.
Voie des rituels|1|desc: By immersing a personal item belonging to a target (a lock of hair suffices) in boiling water and performing the ritual, the PC may see through the target's eyes by observing the water's surface, as long as it is kept at the proper temperature. It may also hear surrounding sounds, though these may experience some distortion or echo.
Voie des rituels|2|desc: The ritual enchants a vessel containing water (the equivalent of a cauldron). Until the next full moon, the water will be enchanted. The PC may carry the equivalent of a flask and may, once per day and regardless of distance, renew the ritual by pouring the liquid into a vase, dipping its fingers in, and mentally communicating with all persons who remained around the original water source.
Voie des rituels|3|desc: After completing the ritual, no animal, material magical creature, or immaterial creature may enter a circle of 20 m in diameter around the PC. Furthermore, no spell may be cast from within or into this zone. For the ritual to remain active, the PC must remain motionless and focused. It is disconnected from the world and does not perceive its surroundings unless physically touched by something, which breaks the trance.
Voie des rituels|4|desc: To perform this ritual, the PC must have an intimate object belonging to a target. A precious jewel or a simple lock of hair may suffice. The PC then takes on the appearance of its target for the next six hours. It retains its own stats and equipment, but whether it is physical appearance, voice, or scent, nothing allows it to be distinguished from the target — not even by divination spells.
Voie des arcanes|0|desc: The PC becomes invisible for [1d6 + WIS Mod.] minutes. Once invisible, no one can detect its presence or attack it. If the PC attacks or casts a spell, it becomes visible again.
Voie des arcanes|1|desc: For [1d6 + WIS Mod.] turns, the PC's metabolism accelerates. Starting from the following turn, it gains an additional action per turn: either a normal attack or a movement action. It can still only perform one limited action per turn, in addition to this extra action.
Voie des arcanes|2|desc: The PC generates a bolt along a 10-meter line. All creatures on the path are subjected to a magical attack test and take [5d6 + INT Mod.] DMG on a failure. Targets may attempt a DEX test with difficulty [12 + INT Mod.] to take only half the DMG.
Voie des arcanes|3|desc: The PC disappears and reappears at another location within [INT × 10] meters. The destination must either be in line of sight or perfectly known by the PC. Alternatively, after a one-hour ritual, the PC may teleport to a distance of [INT × 10] kilometers. The destination must be flat and uninhabited to avoid any accident.
Voie des arcanes|4|desc: The PC projects a lethal ray with a range of 20 meters that negates the cohesion of matter, leaving behind only a pile of dust. A successful magical attack test allows it to hit a creature and deal [6d6 + INT Mod.] DMG. If the PC targets an object carried by a creature, the attack roll suffers a -5 penalty. Traditional and legendary objects are immune to this spell and normal objects are reduced to dust. No object weighing more than 50 kg can be affected: trying to dig a tunnel this way is futile. However, a door (or even a stone in a wall) can be disintegrated this way.
Voie des arts primitifs|0|desc: The PC uses a small fetish (bird skull, pebble, statuette) on which they drip a little of their blood to confer power upon it. This ritual involves the temporary sacrifice of 1 HP. The fetish then allows the character wearing the gris-gris to reroll 1d20 at a moment of their choice within a 12-hour window. The gris-gris loses its power after this delay. By making the HP loss permanent (max HP reduced by 1), the gris-gris allows the reroll of 1d20 once per day, every day. If destroyed, the character regains their HP. The PC can create a maximum of [rank achieved in this path] gris-gris.
Voie des arts primitifs|1|desc: The PC chooses a target. That target's shadow attacks its owner for [3 + WIS Mod.] turns (range 20 m) if a magic attack test succeeds. The shadow pursues its target wherever it flees. Shadow: 1 attack per turn, att = target's att, DMG = target's DMG divided by two.
Voie des arts primitifs|2|desc: By sacrificing 1d4 HP, the PC gains the ability to cast a spell more quickly. A spell requiring an attack action can be cast as a movement action, for example, while a spell requiring concentration can be cast as an attack action instead of a limited action. With this method, if the PC wishes to cast two spells in the same turn, they must sacrifice an additional 1d4 HP (for a maximum of 3d4 HP). Spells affected by this ability are limited to rank 3.
Voie des arts primitifs|3|desc: The PC summons tormented spirits that howl and torment an enemy for a number of turns equal to their WIS Mod. During the spell's duration, the target struggles to focus and cannot perform any limited action unless they succeed on a WIS difficulty 15 test each turn. Additionally, this necrotic storm automatically deals 1d6 DMG each turn.
Voie des arts primitifs|4|desc: As long as the PC stares at their target with the evil eye, that target is burdened with misfortune. In addition to their normal actions, the character can place the evil eye on a visible target of their choice each turn. The chosen victim suffers a -2 penalty to all tests. If they roll a 1 or 2 on the d20 while under the evil eye effect, the player can decide, in agreement with the GM, the extremely unpleasant consequences this critical failure has for the target. If it is a minor creature or NPC, such a result typically leads to their death in a grotesque and/or horrible manner.
Voie des cristaux|0|desc: The PC learns to create a crystal of their choice. They can craft each crystal by spending 200 silver pieces.
Voie des cristaux|1|desc: The PC learns to create a crystal of their choice. They can benefit from the effects of two crystals simultaneously, but not two of the same kind.
Voie des cristaux|2|desc: The PC learns to create two new crystals of their choice. They can benefit from the effects of up to three crystals simultaneously, but not two of the same kind. Swapping a crystal counts as a limited action.
Voie des cristaux|3|desc: The PC learns to create two new crystals of their choice. They can benefit from the effects of up to four crystals simultaneously, but not two of the same kind.
Voie des cristaux|4|desc:
The PC learns to create two new crystals of their choice. They can benefit from the effects of up to five crystals simultaneously, with a maximum of two of the same kind. 
| Color | Shape | Effect |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Milky White | Spindle | Regeneration (1 HP/hour)*|
| Glowing Blue | Sphere | +2 bonus to WIS |
| Midnight Blue | Rhombus | +5 bonus to Init. |
| Pale Blue | Rhombus | +2 bonus to STR |
| Iridescent | Spindle | Allows subsisting without breathing |
| Smoky Black | Prism | Fire and cold resistance 10 points |
| Orange | Spindle | Acid and electricity resistance 10 points |
| Pink and Green | Sphere | +2 bonus to CHA |
| Milky Pink | Prism | +2 bonus to DEF (precognition) |
| Vivid Pink | Rhombus | +2 bonus to CON |
| Red and Blue | Sphere | +2 bonus to INT |
| Blood Red | Sphere | +2 bonus to DEX |
| Translucent | Spindle | Allows subsisting without drinking or eating |
| Pale Green | Prism | +1 bonus to attack (all) |

* The regeneration crystal can only heal HP lost while it was being worn.
Voie des éléments|0|desc: The PC is one with the earth. They can no longer be knocked down and automatically detect all traps set into the ground (trapdoors, pits). They gain a +5 bonus to all tests to detect traps or hidden passages concealed in stone walls or the floor.
Voie des éléments|1|desc: The PC can walk on water at will (as if it were solid ground). They can transfer this ability to others as a limited action by touching the target. They can thus affect a maximum number of targets equal to the rank achieved in this path for a duration of [1d6 + WIS Mod.] minutes.
Voie des éléments|2|desc: The PC can breathe in any hostile environment, underwater as well as in a poisoned or airless environment. They can transfer this ability to others as a limited action by touching the target. They can thus affect a maximum number of targets equal to the rank achieved in this path for a duration of [1d6 + WIS Mod.] minutes.
Voie des éléments|3|desc: The PC becomes immune to fire DMG and now takes only half DMG from cold.
Voie des éléments|4|desc:
* The PC can transform their body once per day for each mastered element for [5 + WIS Mod.] minutes. Each elemental form allows them to subtract [rank achieved in this path] points from all DMG taken (DR) and grants the following abilities.
**==- Fire:==** the character adds +2d6 fire DMG to all melee attacks. A creature that attacks them with melee weapons takes 1d6 DMG for each successful attack.
**==- Water:==** the character heals all wounds at a rate of 3 HP per turn and can deform their body to pass through the smallest gap.
**==- Earth:==** the character gains a +6 bonus to STR (+3 attack, DMG and STR tests) and DEF.
**==- Air:==** the character can fly (at a speed of 30 meters per movement action) and all physical DMG dealt or received are divided by two (subtract DR points afterward).
Voie de la glace|0|desc: A slippery ice sheet covers the ground over an area of 10 m in diameter for the rest of combat (the PC can end the spell at any time). In this zone, remaining standing requires a DEX difficulty 10 test each turn, while moving or fighting within it requires a difficulty 15 test. Failure means the creature falls to the ground. Standing up requires passing a new DEX difficulty 15 test and takes one turn. Crawling out of the zone takes a full turn.
Voie de la glace|1|desc: The PC halves all cold and electricity DMG taken for the rest of combat (10 turns outside of combat).
Voie de la glace|2|desc: The spell affects all creatures in an approximate cone of range 20 meters and width 10 meters at its far end. Victims take [2d6 + INT Mod.] cold DMG and are slowed for 1 turn if they fail a CON difficulty [10 + path rank] test. On a success, they take only half the DMG and are not slowed. The spell's DMG increases to 4d6 upon reaching rank 5 in this path.
Voie de la glace|3|desc: The PC transforms their body into living ice. They gain +4 DEF, are immune to cold, and halve fire DMG. Creatures that touch them or attack them with melee weapons take 1d6 DMG. When they walk, they freeze the ground and can move across water by turning it to ice. The spell lasts [1d6 + WIS Mod.] minutes.
Voie de la glace|4|desc: The PC chooses a target located within 40 meters. They make a magic attack test and compare it to the DEF of all characters (including the PC and their companions) within a radius of 10 meters around the target. Each victim for which the test succeeds takes [5d6 + INT Mod.] cold DMG and is slowed for 1 turn unless they succeed on a CON difficulty 13 test. On a success, they take only half the DMG and are not slowed.
Voie de la métamorphose|0|desc:
The PC can take the form of a small animal (giant eagle, monkey, dog, etc.) or medium animal (wolf, constrictor snake, crocodile, shark, etc.). They retain their HP and their INT, WIS and CHA scores but acquire the following stats:
==**Small Animal Form:** STR -2 DEX +4 * CON -2. DEF 15 Init. 19. Attack +4 DMG 1d4-2
**Medium Animal Form:** STR +1 DEX +1 CON +1. DEF 14 Init. 12. Attack +2 DMG 1d6+1==
They retain their equipment, which is transformed into fur, but the latter does not affect the stats of the animal form. The PC gains one or more natural abilities related to the form taken: flight, keen smell, aquatic breathing, cold resistance, etc. Unless a special ability such as Detect Magic is used, nothing distinguishes the animal form from an ordinary animal. Finally, the PC can return to their humanoid form whenever they wish as a limited action.
Voie de la métamorphose|1|desc: The PC only needs a movement action to return to their humanoid form. After transforming for the first time, they can change form as a movement action — but must still pay the spell's MP cost.
Voie de la métamorphose|2|desc:
The PC can take the form of a large animal (bear, bison, tiger, griffon, shargren, etc.).
==**Large Animal Form:** STR +8* DEX +0 CON +8. DEF 18 Init. 10. Attack +10 DMG 2d6+8==
In this form, the PC gains a +5 bonus to all tests to impress or intimidate.
Voie de la métamorphose|3|desc: If they use a limited action to return to their humanoid form after having taken damage in animal form, the PC recovers 3d6 HP. These HP cannot exceed the DMG received while in animal form.
Voie de la métamorphose|4|desc:
The PC can take the form of a huge animal (elephant, Akrähyng dragon, giant crocodile, etc.).
==**Huge Animal Form:** STR +11* DEX +0 CON +11*. DEF 20 Init. 10. Attack +15 DMG 3d6+11==
In this form, the PC gains a DMG reduction of 2 against all types of DMG.
Voie des vermines|0|desc: The PC acquires the Insect Swarm ability (rank 2 of the path of druidic arts). If they already have this spell, or come to acquire it later, the continuous DMG and test penalty are each increased by 1.
Voie des vermines|1|desc:
The PC adopts a giant scorpion or a giant spider (player's choice).
==**Giant Scorpion:** medium size. Init. 12, DEF 16, HP [4 x PC level], melee attack = [PC level + 3], DMG 1d6+3 [poison 2d6 DMG, CON 13 test = DMG/2], STR +3, DEX +1, CON +3, INT -3, WIS +3, CHA -3 **Giant Spider:** medium size. Init. 16, DEF 15, HP [4 x PC level], melee attack = [PC level + 3], DMG 1d6+1 [poison 3d6 DMG, CON 13 test = DMG/2], STR +1, DEX +3, CON +2, INT -3, WIS +4, CHA -3==
Voie des vermines|2|desc: After years spent in the company of crawling, stinging creatures, the PC has developed an aptitude for controlling their poison. If they use a slashing or piercing weapon, such as a dagger or spear, they can coat it with poison. On a successful melee attack, the PC deals an additional 1d6 DMG at the start of each turn of the affected creature. After suffering the poison effects, the victim can make a CON difficulty [10 + PC's INT Mod.] test to stop the poison. The poison effect does not stack, and a successful CON test grants immunity to the poison for 24 hours. In addition to the effects described above, the PC now gains a +5 bonus to all CON tests to resist poison.
Voie des vermines|3|desc:
The PC's vermin companion gains one of the following abilities, depending on its nature.
**==Grapple (scorpion):==** the scorpion can now attempt to seize a creature in its claws, provided it is not larger than itself. When it rolls 15 to 20 on its attack die, the scorpion grabs its opponent. At the start of the target's turn, the seized creature must make a STR test in opposition to the scorpion; on a success, it manages to break free, otherwise it is stunned (no action and -5 DEF) and the scorpion maintains the hold. While maintaining the hold, the scorpion cannot do anything during its turn, but it automatically deals [1d6+3] DMG to the trapped creature. The scorpion can release its hold whenever it wishes.
**==Web (spider):==** the spider gains the ability to shoot (range: 10 meters) a sticky web at an enemy. If it succeeds on a ranged attack [PC level + 3], the target is immobilized (no movement, use d12: see "Conditions") for 1d6 turns. At the start of the ensnared creature's turn, it must make a STR difficulty 13 test: on a success, it manages to break free, otherwise it remains immobilized.
Voie des vermines|4|desc: The PC earns the title of "Vermin Master." They can now communicate with vermin of all kinds, and those creatures regard them as a friendly creature — unless they are under the control of another more powerful mystic or under magical control. In addition to this communication ability, the PC can now mount their animal companion. The creature takes its normal actions (movement and/or attack) and the PC can act normally from their position (limited action or attack action).
Voie du peuple elfe|0|desc: The PC gains +5 to all CHA tests and silent movement tests.
Voie du peuple elfe|1|desc: The PC is naturally attuned to the essence of the world. They gain a +2 bonus to DEF against magical attacks and to all tests to resist magic. This bonus increases to +4 upon reaching rank 4 in this path.
Voie du peuple elfe|2|desc: The PC has learned to handle the refined craftsmanship of their people. They can wear and use weapons and armor of the elven tradition, for which they possess martial training. See the margin note ==*Traditional Weapons and Armor*==.
