Voie du peuple elfe|3|desc: The PC instinctively knows the direction to take to find the people they hold dear, regardless of the distance that separates them. Moreover, they sense, as a form of premonition, their physical and psychological health. Finally, they also perceive whether a danger threatens them.
Voie du peuple elfe|4|desc: The PC increases their CON score by +2 and their wounds heal faster. When they spend 1 RP, they regain [2 x HD + level + CON Mod.].
Voie culturelle des Elfes bleus: Cultural Path of the Blue Elves
Voie culturelle des Elfes bleus|0|desc: Accustomed to the fluctuations of the waves, the PC has acquired exceptional coordination. Standing up is a free action for them, and they gain a +5 bonus to balance tests.
Voie culturelle des Elfes bleus|1|desc: The PC chooses a caste, religious or warrior. Traditionally, female individuals choose the former, male individuals the latter, but the new generation ignores these conventions. The choice is entirely open. They then gain a rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of divination or the path of mysticism for the religious caste, or a rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of war or the path of two-weapon combat for the warrior caste. At rank 4 of this cultural path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from their caste (path of divination or path of mysticism, path of war or path of two-weapon combat).
Voie culturelle des Elfes bleus|2|desc: The PC gains a +5 bonus to tests to resist mental effects (such as fear, intimidation, or enchantment spells). In addition, they gain a +3 bonus to Initiative.
Voie culturelle des Elfes bleus|3|desc: The PC can hold their breath underwater for 15 minutes. Moreover, in the depths of the oceans, they do not need sunlight to navigate. They can also endure freezing temperatures and pressure without issue.
Voie culturelle des Elfes bleus|4|desc: The PC increases their CON and WIS scores by +2.
Voie culturelle des Elfes sylvains: Cultural Path of the Sylvan Elves
Voie culturelle des Elfes sylvains|0|des: The PC gains a +5 bonus to DEX and WIS tests when in a forest.
Voie culturelle des Elfes sylvains|1|des: The PC chooses a caste, religious or warrior. Traditionally, female individuals choose the former, male individuals the latter, but the new generation ignores these conventions. The choice is entirely open. The Elf then gains a rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of druidic arts or the path of forests for the religious caste, or a rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of archery and the path of the animal companion for the warrior caste. At rank 4 of this cultural path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from their caste (path of druidic arts or path of forests, path of archery or path of the animal companion).
Voie culturelle des Elfes sylvains|2|des: When using a bow, the PC achieves a critical hit on a roll of 19-20 on the d20. They gain proficiency with ranged weapons.
Voie culturelle des Elfes sylvains|3|des: The PC has forged a special bond with an animal that fights by their side. They gain rank 3 of the animal companion path (see page 130). If they already have this ability — or come to acquire it later — their companion gains a +2 bonus to initiative, DEF, attack, and DMG.
Voie culturelle des Elfes sylvains|4|des: The PC increases their DEX and WIS scores by +2.
Voie culturelle des Elfes blancs: Cultural Path of the White Elves
Voie culturelle des Elfes blancs|0|desc: The PC gains a +5 bonus to INT tests related to knowledge and scholarship.
Voie culturelle des Elfes blancs|1|desc: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of enchantment or the path of elemental magic. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of enchantment or the path of elemental magic.
Voie culturelle des Elfes blancs|2|desc: The PC ignores penalties related to using polearm weapons. They gain proficiency with polearms. They also gain a +1 bonus to attack tests and DMG with a polearm.
Voie culturelle des Elfes blancs|3|desc: The PC only needs half the rest, food, or drink of a normal Elf to be in peak condition. They are immune to the effects of poisons and diseases.
Voie culturelle des Elfes blancs|4|desc: The PC increases their INT and WIS scores by +2.
Voie culturelle des Elfes noirs: Cultural Path of the Dark Elves
Voie culturelle des Elfes noirs|0|desc: To conceal their activities, in most civilized countries, Dark Elves pose as artists. They use this cover to obtain the information necessary to fulfill their contracts. The PC chooses a domain (dance, theater, painting, poetry) and gains a +5 bonus to all CHA tests related to it, as well as to tests aimed at finding or obtaining secret or sensitive information.
Voie culturelle des Elfes noirs|1|desc: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of assassination or the path of dark knowledge. At rank 4 of this cultural path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of assassination or the path of dark knowledge.
Voie culturelle des Elfes noirs|2|desc: The PC has learned the art and technique of concealing their weapons and delivering unstoppable lethal strikes. They can conceal up to two light weapons (d6 max) and gain a +5 bonus to a DEX test to oppose a WIS test from their opponent. On a success, the first attack delivered by each weapon catches the opponent off guard and deals maximum DMG (including bonus dice DMG).
Voie culturelle des Elfes noirs|3|desc: The darkness that flows through the Dark Elf's veins is a burden, but can also prove life-saving in the worst moments. Once per combat, when they fall to 0 HP, the PC can continue to act normally, but a single successful attack dealing at least 1 point of DMG will finish them off. While at 0 HP, they gain a +2 bonus to all tests.
Voie culturelle des Elfes noirs|4|desc: The PC increases their DEX and CHA scores by +2.
Voie du peuple nain|0|desc: The PC gains a +5 bonus to all CON tests.
Voie du peuple nain|1|desc: The PC reduces all DMG taken by 1 point (but still takes at least 1 point of DMG per attack received). This bonus stacks with other sources of DMG reduction.
Voie du peuple nain|2|desc: The PC has learned to harness the power of runes engraved by the artisans of the Forge order. They can wear and use weapons and armor of the dwarven tradition for which they possess martial training. See the margin note ==*Traditional Weapons and Armor*==, page 71.
Voie du peuple nain|3|desc: Whenever the PC is the target of a spell (except a zone spell), the player rolls 1d4: on a result higher than the spell's rank, they can ignore its effects.
Voie du peuple nain|4|desc: Once per day, when the PC falls to 0 HP, they can immediately spend 1 RP and apply its effects. In addition, they increase their WIS score by +2.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre de la Forge: Cultural Path of the Forge Order
Voie culturelle de l'ordre de la Forge|0: The PC chooses a trade practiced within the Forge order (alchemist, armorer, physician, goldsmith, miner, etc.). They gain a +5 bonus to all tests related to this trade.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre de la Forge|1: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of alchemy or the path of runic magic. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of alchemy or the path of runic magic.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre de la Forge|2: The PC gains a +1 bonus to attack and DMG when wielding a quarterstaff, war hammer, or two-handed hammer.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre de la Forge|3: The PC engraves protective runes on all of their equipment, and sometimes even on their skin. They gain a +2 bonus to DEF.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre de la Forge|4: The PC increases their DEX and INT scores by +2.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Talion: Cultural Path of the Talion Order
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Talion|0|d: The PC is never short of cash and receives 25 silver pieces (sp) per day for their daily expenses for each rank achieved in this path. If not spent, this money is not counted toward the character's savings.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Talion|1|d: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of the bohemian or the path of trickery. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the path of the bohemian or the path of trickery.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Talion|2|d: By paying the right price, the PC can generally get what they want. When they fail a CHA test, they can gain a +1 bonus for every 10 sp they pay (up to a maximum of 100 sp) to turn their failure into a success.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Talion|3|d:
The PC, by virtue of their wealth and importance within the order, is now protected by a guard. The latter confers a +3 DEF bonus to their employer when at their side. Once per turn, the guard can negate an attack targeting the PC by succeeding on an attack test with a result at least equal to the attacker's attack roll. The guard is completely loyal to the PC. If they die, a new guard can be hired by spending 500 sp or upon advancing to the next level.
==**The Guard:** DEF 16, HP [PC level x 6], attack [PC level], DMG 1d8+2. STR +2, DEX +0, CON +2, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +0. They act at the PC's initiative.==
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Talion|4|d: The PC increases their INT and CHA scores by +2.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Temple: Cultural Path of the Temple Order
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Temple|0|d: The PC replaces brute force with reflection. They can make an INT check instead of a STR check (example: they use a lever to move a heavy load). They gain a +5 bonus to all tinkering or science checks.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Temple|1|d: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the Weapon Mastery path or the Mysticism path. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the Weapon Mastery path or the Mysticism path.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Temple|2|d: The PC invents — or receives as a gift from a fellow engineer — a repeating crossbow. They gain ranged weapons proficiency — including proficiency with this weapon.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Temple|3|d: The PC can sense the souls of the deceased and communicate with them. The ritual requires five minutes of concentration. The exchange can take various forms: a direct dialogue, the use of a written medium, or reading the vibrations of a pool of stagnant water, for example. The deceased always express themselves in an instinctive and confused manner, and it can be tricky to untangle truth from allegory or nightmare. Additionally, the PC can attempt to drive away vengeful or aggressive spirits: at the cost of a limited action and by succeeding on an INT check of difficulty 15, they inflict 2d6 DMG to all spirits in the area.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Temple|4|d: The PC increases their CON and INT values by +2.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Bouclier: Cultural Path of the Shield Order
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Bouclier|0: When the PC fights the same creature as an ally, they gain +1 to attack and DEF for each ally in contact with both themselves and the creature.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Bouclier|1: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the Bastion path or the Command path. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the Bastion path or the Command path.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Bouclier|2: When the PC wields a shield, it provides an additional +1 DEF bonus. Additionally, once per turn, the PC can deflect a projectile (arrow, javelin…) and thus completely ignore a ranged attack that would normally have hit them, unless the attack roll is a critical.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Bouclier|3: The PC always holds their position, to the death. When an opponent in contact with them uses a movement action to move away, they can immediately perform a contact attack as a free action. If successful, the attack deals no DMG but the opponent's movement is cancelled.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Bouclier|4: The PC increases their STR and CHA values by +2.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre des Errants: Cultural Path of the Wanderers' Order
Voie culturelle de l'ordre des Errants|0: The PC suffers a -5 penalty to social interaction checks with high society people, regardless of their people. However, they gain a +5 bonus to checks with the destitute, again regardless of their people. They also gain a +5 bonus to stealth checks and people generally do not remember them.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre des Errants|1: The PC may not have always been outside an order, or perhaps received teachings from a fallen Dwarf? They can choose a rank 1 ability from the Alchemy path, the Runic Magic path, the Bohemian path, the Trickery path, the Weapon Mastery path, the Mysticism path, the Bastion path, or the Command path. At rank 4 of this path, they can choose a new rank 2 ability from the Alchemy path, the Runic Magic path, the Bohemian path, the Trickery path, the Weapon Mastery path, the Mysticism path, the Bastion path, or the Command path. These abilities cannot become superior abilities.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre des Errants|2: The PC gains a +1 bonus to attack and DMG when wielding a peasant weapon. Additionally, they are proficient with the woodcutter's greataxe (see page 157).
Voie culturelle de l'ordre des Errants|3: Wanderers and the destitute instinctively recognize each other and sometimes show great self-sacrifice for their own kind. When another destitute person has the opportunity to help the PC, roll 1d6. On 1 to 4, the NPC provides total and unconditional help (without expecting payment and regardless of the danger). They can use their own contacts and allies to complete the task if necessary, but these intermediaries may have demands the PCs will need to meet. However, the GM also rolls 1d6 secretly: on a 6, the NPC is actually plotting a dirty trick to make some money…
Voie culturelle de l'ordre des Errants|4: The PC increases their CON and WIS values by +2.
Voie du peuple humain|0|desc: After failing a STAT check, the PC gains a +5 bonus on the next check to attempt the same action on the next turn.
Voie du peuple humain|1|desc: The PC gains +1 to DMG when fighting a humanoid opponent. This bonus increases to +2 at rank 4 of this path.
Voie du peuple humain|2|desc: The PC chooses one of the martial proficiencies their character has mastered as well as a tradition from one of the peoples of the Lands of Arran: the PC can wield and use only the weapons or armor they are proficient with, forged in this tradition. See the margin note *==Traditional Weapons and Armor,==* page 71.
Voie du peuple humain|3|desc: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from any profile path.
Voie du peuple humain|4|desc: The PC increases the value of a STAT of their choice by +2.
Voie culturelle des ruines nordiques: Cultural Path of the Northern Ruins
Voie culturelle des ruines nordiques|0|d: The PC gains a +5 bonus to navigation, swimming, and balance checks.
Voie culturelle des ruines nordiques|1|d: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the Bravery path or the Hunting path. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the Bravery path or the Hunting path.
Voie culturelle des ruines nordiques|2|d: The PC gains a +1 bonus to attack and DMG when wielding an axe. Additionally, they are proficient with the woodcutter's greataxe.
Voie culturelle des ruines nordiques|3|d:
The PC has earned such status within their clan that they now have a henchman in their service who fights at their side and covers their back on the battlefield. They are completely loyal to the PC and can also be assigned some menial tasks, such as maintaining weapons or managing supplies. Thanks to them, the PC's weapons are perfectly maintained and inflict criticals on a result of 19 or 20.
==**Right Hand: **DEF 16, HP [PC level x 6], attack [PC level], DMG 1d8+2. STR +2, DEX +0, CON +2, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +0. Acts at the PC's initiative.==
If they were to die, the PC could take another right-hand man into service at the next level.
Voie culturelle des ruines nordiques|4|d: The PC increases their DEX and CON values by +2.
Voie culturelle du Mitan|0|desc: The PC is comfortable in crowds and bustling cities. They gain a +5 bonus to all perception-related checks in a crowd, as well as for finding a person's address or the location of a specific place in an urban area.
Voie culturelle du Mitan|1|desc: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the Mounted Combat path or the Crossbow Shooting path. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the Mounted Combat path or the Crossbow Shooting path.
Voie culturelle du Mitan|2|desc: The PC gains a +1 bonus to attack and DMG when wielding a crossbow. Additionally, they gain ranged weapons proficiency.
Voie culturelle du Mitan|3|desc: The PC knows many people in the Lands of Arran. Once per adventure, they can call upon a friend or contact to obtain a service, information, or seal an alliance. To do so, they must succeed on a CHA check of difficulty 10 if the service is routine, 15 if the service is difficult or may pose a risk to the contact, and 20 if the service involves secret information or openly endangers the ally. If the check fails, the friend may still grant their help, but it won't be free…
Voie culturelle du Mitan|4|desc: The PC increases their INT and CHA values by +2.
Voie culturelle des empires austraux: Cultural Path of the Southern Empires
Voie culturelle des empires austraux|0|d: The PC can read and write and gains +5 to checks related to a domain of their choice: history and geography, occultism and magic, science and technology, plants and creatures, or ancient languages.
Voie culturelle des empires austraux|1|d: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the Elemental Magic path or the Illusions path. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the Elemental Magic path or the Illusions path.
Voie culturelle des empires austraux|2|d: The PC exudes an impression of confidence, wisdom, and uncommon strength of character. They gain a +2 bonus to Initiative and DEF. In place of this ability, the player can, if they wish, choose a new domain of scholarship for their PC (rank 1 ability of this path).
Voie culturelle des empires austraux|3|d: Through study and experimentation, the PC has greater arcane resources. They add their CHA Mod. to their total MP (this bonus is not doubled for the mystic class).
Voie culturelle des empires austraux|4|d: The PC increases their INT and WIS values by +2.
Voie culturelle des Terres orientales: Cultural Path of the Eastern Lands
Voie culturelle des Terres orientales|0|: The PC is accustomed to negotiating with humanoids from all regions of the world. The customs and practices of the peoples of Arran hold no secrets for them. They gain a +5 bonus to all checks to negotiate, argue, lie, or convince a non-human.
Voie culturelle des Terres orientales|1|: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the Charm path or the Trickery path. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the Charm path or the Trickery path.
Voie culturelle des Terres orientales|2|: The PC has developed for their survival an extraordinary sense for spotting spellcasters — and better evading them! By succeeding on a WIS check of difficulty 15, they immediately sense if their interlocutor is versed in the mystical arts — that is, if they master at least one path from the mystic family or an associated creature path. Additionally, they benefit from a +5 bonus to checks aimed at resisting magical effects.
Voie culturelle des Terres orientales|3|: The PC is passionate about flying mount races. They acquire an Akrähyng dragon with the following stats: Init 15, DEF 16, HP [5 × PC level], Att +8, DMG 1d6+4 (fire breath, range 20 m). If they possess, or come to acquire later, the ability ==*Fantastic Mount*== (Mounted Combat path, rank 5) or ==*Order of Knighthood*== (prestige path of the winged order, rank 1), their mount gains a +2 bonus to DEF, attack, and DMG for each of those abilities. Their mount is perfectly loyal and they gain a +5 bonus to DEX checks for all aerial maneuvers performed with it.
Voie culturelle des Terres orientales|4|: The PC increases their DEX and CHA values by +2.
Voie du peuple des Peaux vertes|0|desc: The Greenskins are pariahs in the eyes of the other peoples of the Lands of Arran but, within their clans, their victories and feats of arms forge their legends. The PC gains a +5 bonus to STAT checks for all social interaction with other Greenskins.
Voie du peuple des Peaux vertes|1|desc: Once per opponent, after missing a contact attack, the PC can announce that this attack was actually a dirty strike: the blow was intended to divert the opponent's attention, giving the PC time to land a vicious hit. After an opposed check whose nature is specified below, the PC can perform one of the following maneuvers: *==blind==* (DEX vs CON), *==disarm==* (STR or DEX vs STR), or *==push back==* (STR vs STR) — see page 110 for more information on the outcome of these maneuvers.
Voie du peuple des Peaux vertes|2|desc:
The PC has learned to make the most of their people's strange traditions. They can wear and use the traditional weapons and armor of their people for which they have martial proficiency. For Aberrations (cf. page 97), the nature of the tradition is defined by the Cultural Path (for example an Aberration with the Ogre cultural path can claim mastery of traditional Ogre weapons).
See the margin note *==Traditional Weapons and Armor,==* page 71.
Voie du peuple des Peaux vertes|3|desc: The PC now sports a mystical tattoo that enhances their physical performance. Choose: bull (+5 to STR checks), bear (+5 to CON checks), panther (+5 to DEX checks), or owl (+5 to WIS checks).
Voie du peuple des Peaux vertes|4|desc: The PC can spend 1 CP to cancel a critical hit against them and turn it into a normal hit. Once per day, they can spend 2 CP to turn a successful check against them into a failure. N.B.: it is not possible to cancel a critical success.
Voie culturelle des Ogres|0|desc: The PC can wield with one hand large weapons that normally require two hands. They suffer a -2 penalty to attack rolls. For traditional Ogre weapons, this penalty is increased by 1 per magic level (an exceptional two-handed weapon imposes a -5 penalty if wielded with one hand).
Voie culturelle des Ogres|1|desc: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the Ferocity path or the Fisticuffs path. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the Ferocity path or the Fisticuffs path.
Voie culturelle des Ogres|2|desc: The PC performs a contact attack with their full weight. They gain a DMG bonus equal to their [CON Mod.] and suffer in return a DEF penalty equal to their [CON Mod.] for one turn.
Voie culturelle des Ogres|3|desc: When about to fall to 0 HP, the PC can choose not to fall unconscious and continue fighting. They can still be wounded, their HP being counted negatively. They can go down to an amount equal to their Constitution value, beyond which they die. As long as their HP are below 0, they cannot benefit from any healing unless they spend 1 RP.
Voie culturelle des Ogres|4|desc: The PC increases their STR and CON values by +2.
Voie culturelle des Orcs|0|desc: The PC gains a +5 bonus to all STR checks and adds their [STR Mod.] to their total HP score.
Voie culturelle des Orcs|1|desc: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the Ferocity path or the Power path. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the Ferocity path or the Power path.
Voie culturelle des Orcs|2|desc: The PC's DMG is multiplied by 3 (instead of 2) when they score a critical success on a contact attack.
Voie culturelle des Orcs|3|desc: The PC performs a violent contact attack that causes hemorrhage. In addition to normal DMG, the attack produces bleeding that inflicts +1d6 DMG at the start of each subsequent turn until the target succeeds on a CON check of difficulty [12 + STR Mod.] or is healed. Multiple bleeding effects cannot be stacked.
Voie culturelle des Orcs|4|desc: The PC increases their STR and CON values by +2.
Voie culturelle des Gobelins|0|desc: The PC has a strange ability to be forgotten or to divert attention. Once per combat, when targeted by an enemy, they can invoke this ability to redirect their attacker's attention to another enemy target. The enemy will no longer target the Goblin, unless the latter attacks them again. Additionally, the Goblin gains a +5 bonus to all checks aimed at blending into a crowd.
Voie culturelle des Gobelins|1|desc: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the Acrobatics path or the Assassination path. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the Acrobatics path or the Assassination path.
Voie culturelle des Gobelins|2|desc: By succeeding on a DEX check of difficulty 10, the PC can move (up to a maximum of 20 m) to a shadowy area or out of sight (behind a wall, in a bush, or under a carriage for example, as long as no one can clearly see the area) and disappear on their turn. They only reappear on the next turn, during their initiative phase. No opponent can attack them while they have disappeared into the shadows, but they can suffer area DMG. The Goblin reappears at a maximum distance of 10 m from their initial position. If they have initiative over an opponent they target in melee, they gain a +2d6 DMG bonus to their attack.
Voie culturelle des Gobelins|3|desc: The PC can move on vertical surfaces with the same ease as on a horizontal surface. They need to use all four limbs to move normally. However, with only three limbs engaged, they can remain still or move at half their normal speed (10 m per movement action).
Voie culturelle des Gobelins|4|desc: The PC increases their DEX and INT values by +2.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Malt: Cultural Path of the Malt Order
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Malt|0|des: The first thing a member of the order learns is the delicate art of brewing and the second is to appreciate the product of their craft. The PC gains a +5 bonus to all craft and knowledge checks related to brewing and alcohol in general. They also benefit from a +5 bonus to CON checks to resist the effects of alcohol.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Malt|1|des: The PC chooses a rank 1 or 2 ability from the Power path or the Charm path. At rank 4 of this path, they gain an additional rank 1 or 2 ability from the Power path or the Charm path.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Malt|2|des: Drinking alcohol for a dwarf of the Malt order is a social activity. The PC gains a +5 bonus to all CHA checks with anyone who agrees to share an alcoholic drink with them.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Malt|3|des: Alcohol abuse is known to dull pain and make one insensible to fear. The PC gains (Rank=__d6 temporary bonus HP for one hour after consuming a significant amount of alcohol. These temporary HP are the first to be lost. After one hour, all remaining temporary HP disappear. During the same duration, the PC becomes insensible to all forms of fear. Some call it courage, others recklessness. During this hour, they can benefit up to three times from a +5 bonus to a STR, CON, or CHA check. This ability can only be used once per day.
Voie culturelle de l'ordre du Malt|4|des: The PC increases their CHA and CON values by +2.
Voie des golems|0|desc:
The runist builds a worker golem, fragile but practical. It has finely articulated hands capable of performing delicate tasks. These can be exchanged for various tools if needed. When it can assist the PC in performing a manual task, it grants them a +2 bonus to a STAT check.
==**Worker Golem:** Init. [PC], DEF 10, HP [3 x level], Attack [Lvl.], DMG [1d4 + STR], STR +1, DEX -1, CON +5, INT -4, WIS -3, CHA -4==
If damaged, the runist can repair the golem at the rate of [1d4 per rank + INT Mod.] HP per hour. After the first hour of work, the PC can infuse their MP into the golem to accelerate restoration at the rate of 1 HP per MP infused.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**Repairing the golem is twice as fast, [1d4 per rank + INT Mod.] HP per 30-minute period. The runist can begin infusing their MP into the golem from 30 minutes of repair.
Voie des golems|1|desc:
In addition to their worker golem, the runist is now capable of building a more resilient golem better suited for combat.
==**War Golem: **Init. [PC], DEF 14, HP [4 x level], Attack [Lvl. +STR], DMG [1d8 +STR], STR +1, DEX -1, CON +10, INT -4, WIS -3, CHA -4==
When designing the golem, the PC defines a role among the following: Soldier, Guardian, or Gladiator.
❑ **==Soldier:==** the golem has a weapon, blades, or claws built into its fists. Its DMG increases to [1d10 + STR].
❑ **==Guardian:==** once per turn, if the golem is in contact with the PC, it can interpose itself and take the DMG in their place.
❑ **==Gladiator:==** when the golem misses a contact attack, it gains a cumulative +1 bonus to DMG and contact attack rolls until it hits.
The runist can modify the golem's role by reworking the runic armor. This requires a workshop, one month of work, and various materials at a total cost of 50 gp (the first role choice is free).
If damaged, the runist can repair the golem at the rate of [1d6 per rank + INT Mod.] HP per hour. After the first hour of repair, the PC can infuse their MP into the golem to accelerate restoration at the rate of 1 HP per MP infused.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**The runist can modify their golem's role more quickly and now only needs one week of work (material costs remain the same).
Voie des golems|2|desc:
The worker golem is capable of manipulating complex objects and receiving orders related to their use. It can for example administer an elixir to an ally or use an unstable scroll. It gains a +3 bonus to its DEX Mod. (as well as to DEF).
Additionally, the war golem acquires the following improvement (depending on its role):
❑ **==Soldier:==** at the cost of a limited action, the golem can attack two creatures in contact with it. A single attack roll is compared to the DEF of each target.
❑ **==Guardian:==** the golem's armor is considerably reinforced, providing DR 3. This DR does not apply against damage of magical origin (spells and magical weapons).
❑ **==Gladiator:==** when the golem is hit by a contact attack, it can retaliate once per turn as an immediate free action against its attacker.
Voie des golems|3|desc:
The runist can improve their war golem by choosing one option from the following:
❑ **==Two-Handed Weapon:==** +1d8 to contact DMG
❑ **==Reinforced Armor:==** DEF +4
❑**== Bestial Form:==** +3 to DEX Mod. (and DEF)
❑ **==Impulse:==** at the cost of a limited action, can jump 20 meters high or far
❑ **==Runic Protection:==** immune to lightning, halves fire and cold DMG
❑ **==Powerful:==** +2 STR Mod.
❑ **==Greater Size:==** +10 HP and +1 STR Mod.
The runist chooses an additional option when reaching levels 9, 13, and 17.
Voie des golems|4|desc: The runist increases their INT value by +2. They can now roll two d20 whenever an INT check is required and keep the best result.
Voie des mutagènes|0|desc:
At the cost of a limited action, the PC activates a mutation that strengthens their body for [10 + WIS Mod.] minutes. Stigmata appear and provide one of the following effects, at the PC's choice:
==❑ Draconic Scales:== DEF +4
==❑  Insulating Fur:== cold and lightning DMG halved
==❑  Copper Cuirass:== Damage Reduction (DR) 3
==❑  Black Blood:== Immunity to poison
**==Advanced Ability: ==**The PC can benefit from the effects of a reinforcement a second time per day.
Voie des mutagènes|1|desc:
At the cost of a limited action, the PC changes their appearance to that of another humanoid for [30 minutes per rank reached in the path]. Physical proportions must be respected with a margin of error of 30 cm in height and width.
The PC can change sex and ethnicity. The tone of their voice is also altered. They can take on the appearance of a specific humanoid, but for this they must possess an ingredient belonging to the target (a lock of hair for example). Preparing such a potion takes approximately 6 hours.
The PC retains their stats and cannot be unmasked by a magic detection spell or equivalent — but their behavior could still betray them.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**The PC can change identity a second time per day.
Voie des mutagènes|2|desc:
The PC improves their body's capabilities by choosing an augmentation from the following list:
==❑==  Bonus of 2 x [PC Lvl.] max HP
==❑ == Bonus of [PC Lvl.] max MP
==❑==  Bonus of +5 to STR checks
==❑==  Bonus of +5 to WIS checks
The augmentation fades after one day. Improvements increasing max HP and MP thresholds are also accompanied by an equivalent gain in HP and MP.
Voie des mutagènes|3|desc:
At the cost of a limited action, the PC considerably accelerates their metabolism for one hour. Their body recovers much faster from exertion, eliminates toxins and poisons more quickly, and heals wounds faster.
They gain a +5 bonus to CON checks and only suffer the effects of poisoning for one turn (instead of their normal duration). During the duration of this potion, when they spend RP to recover HP, the gain formula becomes: [2DV + CON Mod. + Lvl] HP.
Voie des mutagènes|4|desc: The PC chooses a physical characteristic (STR, DEX, or CON): they permanently increase its value by +2.
Voie des pentacles|0|desc:
The PC can touch a weapon and hone its edge for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns, granting a +1 bonus to that weapon's DMG. This pentacle only affects slashing and piercing weapons. Crossbow bolts and arrows can be improved with this spell, at a rate of 5 projectiles per spell.
The bonus increases to +2 at rank 3 in the path, and to +3 at rank 5 in the path.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**The PC can also apply the bonus to melee attack rolls.
Voie des pentacles|1|desc:
The PC's skin transforms into alchemical mercury, granting a +3 bonus to DEF for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns.
**==Advanced Ability:==** While under the effect of this spell, the PC benefits from a DMG Reduction (DR) of 2 against all elemental and magical damage.
Voie des pentacles|2|desc:
The PC can reshape matter and transform it into an object of their choice. The PC can alter the equivalent of 10 kg of raw material per rank reached in the path. To cast the spell, the PC must have raw material to alter (the ground, a wall, an object to transform, etc.). The created object remains unstable and reverts to its initial properties after one hour (it is not possible to eat food created this way, for example).
It is not possible to modify magical or traditional objects this way. And it is strictly forbidden in most countries for alchemists to produce gold or precious gems in this manner.
Voie des pentacles|3|desc:
The PC launches a jet of molten metal at a target within 10 meters. On a successful magical attack roll, it inflicts [2d6 + INT Mod.] DMG. Unless they succeed at a CON test difficulty [12 + INT Mod.], the target continues to suffer 1d6 burning DMG per turn for [WIS Mod.] turn(s) (minimum 1).
The same target can accumulate multiple burn DMG effects.
Voie des pentacles|4|desc:
Once per turn as an immediate action, the PC can counter an attack by creating a force field that can take the form of a shield, a wall, or a dome 4 meters in radius. They make a magical attack roll and gain temporary DMG Reduction (DR) equal to the result of their roll. The dome dissipates immediately after the countered attack.
This spell cannot benefit from concentration and the alchemist can only take a movement action on their next turn.
Voie des homoncules|0|desc:
As a limited action, the PC brings to life a humanoid creature with the following stats:
**==Init. 10, DEF 12, HP [4 x PC's Lvl.].
Melee attack (fists) = [PC's lvl.], DMG 1d6 + 1
STR +1, DEX +0, CON +4, INT -4, WIS -3, CHA -4==**
In this form, it can manipulate objects and even use weapons (but it is not trained in their use and suffers a -3 penalty to rolls).
At rank 4 in this path, the homunculus's melee attacks can — in any form — benefit from the effects of a honing spell.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The PC can add their [INT Mod.] to the homunculus's max HP. This bonus is doubled at rank 4 in the path.
Voie des homoncules|1|desc:
As long as the homunculus is not destroyed (HP = 0), the PC can repair it by touching it, as an attack action. For each MP spent, the homunculus regains 1d4 HP.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The PC can use this ability from up to 20 meters away.
Voie des homoncules|2|desc:
As a limited action, the homunculus can transform into a medium-sized quadruped beast resembling a dog or large feline. Its stats become the following (HP remain the same):
**==Init. 16, DEF 16, Melee attack (bite and claws) = [PC's lvl. +2], DMG 1d6 + 1
STR +0, DEX +3, CON +4, INT -4, WIS -3, CHA -4==**
In this form, the homunculus moves 30 meters per movement action and can charge its enemies.
**==Charge (L):==** The creature moves in a straight line for at least 6 meters (30 meters maximum) and makes a melee attack with a +2 bonus to attack and +1d6 DMG.
Voie des homoncules|3|desc:
The PC uses a pentacle on their homunculus (range 20 meters maximum), granting it an illusory appearance compatible with its current form (for example a humanoid appearance for the humanoid homunculus). The illusion persists as long as the homunculus does not change form or is not touched.
The alchemist can cast the spell on themselves or an ally, but the illusion then only lasts [5 + WIS Mod.] turns.
The spell does not allow taking on a precise appearance unless the alchemist knows the copied creature intimately.
Voie des homoncules|4|desc:
As a limited action, the homunculus can transform into a large ground mount (horse-type) or aerial mount (dragon-type). Its stats become the following (HP remain the same):
**==Init. 12, DEF 15, Melee attack = [PC's lvl.], DMG 1d6+4
STR +4, DEX +1, CON +4, INT -4, WIS -3, CHA -4==**
In this form, the homunculus can carry up to two humanoids and travels 30 meters per movement action (whether on the ground or in the air).
Voie des pierres alchimiques|0|desc:
The PC chooses an ability from the novice alchemical stones list.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The PC chooses an additional ability from the novice alchemical stones list.
Voie des pierres alchimiques|1|desc:
The PC chooses an ability from the novice alchemical stones list.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The PC chooses an additional ability from the novice alchemical stones list.
Voie des pierres alchimiques|2|desc: The PC chooses an ability from the initiate alchemical stones list.
Voie des pierres alchimiques|3|desc: Using novice soul stones no longer requires more than a movement action.
Voie des pierres alchimiques|4|desc: The PC chooses an ability from the initiate alchemical stones list.
Voie des pentacles interdits|0|desc:
The PC must succeed at a magical attack roll (range 20 meters). On success, the target is petrified and turned to stone for [2d6 + WIS Mod.] turns. In this form, it feels nothing, cannot act, and its DEF drops to 10. It reduces all DMG taken by 30 points (DR 30).
Affected creatures must succeed at a CON test difficulty [16 + INT Mod.] to reduce the effect's duration to 1 turn. A creature can only be subjected to this pentacle once per day.
Voie des pentacles interdits|1|desc:
The PC alters light to form shards of pure energy. They make a magical attack roll and compare it to the DEF of all targets around them within a 6-meter radius. On success, they suffer [2d6 + INT Mod.] magical DMG and are blinded for 1 turn. On failure, targets only take half the DMG and are not blinded.
The alchemist must have a light source available. They cannot use this ability in complete darkness.
Voie des pentacles interdits|2|desc: The PC can form two pentacles at the same time. The cost of each spell must be paid individually and they cannot benefit from concentration.
Voie des pentacles interdits|3|desc:
Once per day, the PC touches a target and casts a powerful pentacle that closes all wounds by regenerating destroyed organs and limbs. The target recovers all their lost HP and is cured of any poison, disease, detrimental condition, and disease weakening.
Unfortunately, destroyed organs and limbs that have already healed before the pentacle is used cannot be regenerated this way.
Voie des pentacles interdits|4|desc:
The PC touches a target and generates an antimatter field that destroys everything it contacts. On a successful magical attack roll, it inflicts [3d8 + INT Mod.] magical DMG. This attack ignores the target's DR.
If the attack fails but the target is wearing manufactured armor or a shield, the pentacle destroys part of their equipment and they suffer a -2 penalty to DEF.
Voie des pierres supérieures|0|desc: The PC chooses an ability from the master alchemical stones list.
Voie des pierres supérieures|1|desc: As a limited action, the PC can use two initiate and/or master alchemical stones, or three novice alchemical stones.
Voie des pierres supérieures|2|desc: The PC chooses an ability from the master alchemical stones list.
Voie des pierres supérieures|3|desc: The PC chooses an ability from the divine soul stones list. They can use one divine soul stone per day. Divine stones always require a limited action to use.
Voie des pierres supérieures|4|desc: The PC chooses an ability from the divine soul stones list. They can still only use one divine stone per day. Divine stones always require a limited action to use.
Voie de la mort|0|desc:
The PC makes a magical attack roll against a target (range: 20 m). On success, they torment the target with the screaming voices of deceased spirits, terrifying it and forcing it to flee for [1 + WIS Mod.] turns (minimum 1 turn).
This spell is ineffective on targets without a mind (such as undead or artificial creatures).
**==Advanced Ability:==** At the end of the spell, the target suffers a -2 penalty to its attack rolls for [1 + WIS Mod.] turns (minimum 1 turn).
Voie de la mort|1|desc:
The PC animates the corpse of a medium-sized humanoid who has been dead for less than an hour (range: 20 m). The revenant understands the commands "Attack", "Follow", "Guard", and "Stay".
Revenant: Init 8, DEF 10, HP 12, Attack [PC's Lvl.], DMG [1d6 + INT Mod.], moves at 50% of its normal speed. The revenant degrades and loses 1 HP per minute and cannot be healed.
The PC can control one revenant per rank reached in the path.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**As a limited action by spending 3 additional MP, the PC can animate two corpses during the same turn. They can control a total of [rank reached in the path] x 2 revenants.
Voie de la mort|2|desc: On a successful magical attack roll (range 10 m), the PC causes their victim's flesh to rot, inflicting [2d6 + INT Mod.] DMG. The victim suffers a -2 penalty to all tests for the rest of the combat (cumulative with other putrefaction spells) unless they succeed at a CON test difficulty [13 + INT Mod.]. Succeeding the test cancels all accumulated putrefaction penalties.
Voie de la mort|3|desc:
For [5 + WIS Mod.] turns, the PC's features take on the appearance of a corpse. Their heart stops beating and they stop breathing, making them immune for the duration of the spell to the effects of asphyxiation, poison, bleeding, or diseases. Additionally, they reduce all physical DMG by 2 points and halve cold DMG.
In this form, they can no longer be healed.
Voie de la mort|4|desc:
The PC designates a 20-meter diameter zone (range: 50 m) and causes an army of skeletons to rise from the ground, immediately assaulting enemies present for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns. The army automatically inflicts [3d6 + INT Mod.] DMG per turn unless enemies use a limited action (L) to oppose the dead (in which case they only take 1d6 DMG). Movement in the area is halved (even for allies).
The army can move 10 meters each turn as long as it stays within the spell's range.
The spell requires the PC's full concentration: while maintaining it, they can only perform one action per turn (move or attack — they can no longer take a limited action).
Each time they take DMG, they must succeed at an INT test with a difficulty equal to the amount of DMG taken or immediately end the spell.
Voie de la vie|0|desc:
The PC must succeed at a magical attack roll (range: 10 m). Blood flows from the mouth, nose, ears, and even the eyes of the victim, who takes 1d6 DMG per turn for [rank reached in the path] turns. This spell ignores a target's DR but creatures that don't bleed are immune (such as non-living creatures, ghouls, or dryads). The DMG increases to 1d8 at rank 4 in this path.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The spell's duration becomes [rank reached in the path + WIS Mod.] turns.
Voie de la vie|1|desc:
Once per turn, when a humanoid creature dies within 20 meters of the PC, they can cast this spell as an immediate free action to regain 1d6 HP (up to their max HP). This spell cannot benefit from concentration.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The gain increases to 1d8 HP.
Voie de la vie|2|desc: The necromancer designates a target (range: 50 m) and makes a magical attack roll. On success, they inflict [1d8 + INT Mod.] DMG and recover that many HP (without exceeding their max HP). Alternatively, they can transfer this vital energy to an allied target they must touch: the ally recovers half the inflicted DMG (rounded up).
Voie de la vie|3|desc: If the PC succeeds at a magical attack roll (range: 10 m), the victim bleeds at the slightest wound. All bleeding attack DMG inflicted on the target (blade, claw, bleeding, etc.) increase by 1d6 for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns. This spell ignores a target's DR but creatures that don't bleed are immune (such as non-living creatures, ghouls, or dryads).
Voie de la vie|4|desc: On a successful magical attack roll (range: 20 m), the PC forges a link with their victim. For [5 + WIS Mod.] turns, half the DMG received by the PC is also suffered by the target, and the PC can cast a spell on them without seeing them.
Voie de la sombre magie|0|desc:
The PC invokes a fixed spherical zone of magical darkness, 10 meters in diameter, lasting [5 + WIS Mod.] turns. Even creatures capable of seeing in the dark are blinded in this zone.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The PC is not completely blinded in the zone, which is merely heavy shadow for them (-5 to ranged attacks).
Voie de la sombre magie|1|desc:
The PC makes a magical attack roll against a target within 20 meters. On success, the victim makes all their tests with two d20 and keeps the lowest result. The spell ends once they have failed three tests — with no time limit. If the victim has an ability allowing them to roll two d20 and keep the best, they now only roll one.
A victim cannot suffer more than one curse per day.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The PC can curse a total of [1 + INT Mod.] targets simultaneously (minimum 1).
Voie de la sombre magie|2|desc:
As a free action, the PC sacrifices 1d4 HP and immediately gains +3 on a d20 roll of their choice or to DEF against an attack. Starting from rank 5 in this path, they can sacrifice 2d4 HP to increase this bonus to +5.
This spell cannot benefit from concentration.
Voie de la sombre magie|3|desc:
The PC designates a target within 20 meters and mimes crushing their heart in their hand. On a successful magical attack roll, the victim immediately suffers [4d6 + INT Mod.] DMG — the spell ignores DR.
They can succeed at a CON test difficulty 14 to halve the DMG.
Voie de la sombre magie|4|desc: The PC increases their INT value by +2. They can now roll two d20 whenever an INT test is required and keep the best result.
Voie des pactes|0|desc:
The PC chooses a creature from the pacts list. They can summon rank 1 pacts.
**==Advanced Ability:==** Summoning a rank 1 pact costs no MP.
Voie des pactes|1|desc:
The PC chooses a creature from the pacts list. They can summon rank 2 pacts.
Advanced Ability: Summoning a rank 2 pact costs only 1 MP.
Voie des pactes|2|desc: The PC chooses a creature from the pacts list. They can summon rank 3 pacts.
Voie des pactes|3|desc: The PC chooses a creature from the pacts list. They can summon rank 4 pacts.
Voie des pactes|4|desc: The PC chooses a creature from the pacts list. They can summon rank 5 pacts.
Voie de la non-mort|0|desc: The PC targets the corpse of a humanoid creature within 30 meters, dead for less than one hour. They violently release the residual spirit from the remains, which escapes screaming, causing the body to explode. The resulting spiritual blast inflicts [2d6 per rank in the path + INT Mod.] DMG in a 10-meter diameter zone centered on the corpse. Victims must succeed at a CHA test difficulty [10 + INT Mod.] to halve the DMG — allies automatically succeed this test.
Voie de la non-mort|1|desc:
For each corpse of a humanoid creature within 20 meters of the PC and dead for less than one hour, they gain a +1 bonus to all attack rolls and DMG.
Remains altered by *spiritual liberation* provide no bonus.
Voie de la non-mort|2|desc:
Once per day, through a 10-minute ritual, the PC can bring back to life a humanoid creature that has been dead for less than one day. They must know the deceased person and sacrifice another humanoid creature in exchange to perform the ritual.
The reanimated creature enters a state of undeath, a form of stasis in which it no longer ages. It still needs to meet its body's minimum vital needs (breathing, eating, drinking) but its metabolism is slowed: it only takes half DMG from poison and cold; it is immune to diseases. In return, it becomes immune to healing abilities and only the use of RP allows it to heal.
If it were to die again, its body would turn to dust, making any new reanimation impossible. The PC can revoke the reanimation whenever they wish.
The PC can maintain up to [INT Mod. - 1] (minimum 1) creatures in a state of reanimation.
Voie de la non-mort|3|desc: The PC creates a 6-meter diameter dome (range: 20 m) that provides a +4 bonus to DEF and to all tests to resist attacks or abilities of undead creatures, creatures from other planes of existence, ghosts or demons, and necromancy spells. The dome remains active for [5 + WIS Mod.] turns.
Voie de la non-mort|4|desc:
On a successful magical attack roll against a humanoid creature (range: 20 m), the PC forces the soul to be extracted from the body and inflicts [2d6 + INT Mod.] DMG per turn. On its turn, the victim must succeed at a STR test opposed by the necromancer's INT: on failure, it cannot act that turn. If the victim dies while under the spell, the PC can immediately cast a Spiritual Liberation spell if desired (the spell consumes no action and cannot benefit from concentration).
The PC can maintain the effects of a disincarnation spell each turn, at the cost of a limited action (L).
Voie des pactes impies|0|desc: The PC chooses a creature from the pacts list. They can summon rank 6 pacts.
Voie des pactes impies|1|desc:
Summoning a creature now only requires an attack action instead of a limited action, which still cannot benefit from concentration.
At rank 4 in the path, summoning a creature only requires a movement action. It is not possible to summon more than one creature per turn, but the PC can use their attack action to perform another action (such as casting a spell for example).
Voie des pactes impies|2|desc: The PC can replace a learned pact with another pact from the list. Additionally, they can summon rank 7 pacts.
Voie des pactes impies|3|desc: There is no longer a limit to how many times a creature can be summoned per day, even if it had to flee or was killed (except for creatures with established limits such as Virrath or Ukmaak).
Voie des pactes impies|4|desc: A pact can be summoned twice at the same time (but on two different turns). Additionally, the necromancer can summon rank 8 pacts.
Voie de la gravure|0|desc:
The runist engraves a series of runes on a staff. With this magical two-handed weapon, they gain a +1 bonus to melee attack rolls and inflict [1d4 + INT Mod.] magical DMG. From rank 3, they inflict [2d4 + INT Mod.] magical DMG. From rank 5, they inflict [4d4 + INT Mod.] magical DMG.
Only the runist can benefit from this weapon's magical effects. If the staff is lost or broken, the runist can engrave runes on another weapon at the cost of a day's work. Traditional or legendary weapons cannot be engraved this way.
**==Advanced Ability: ==**The runist can use their magical attack score to make melee attacks with this staff.
Voie de la gravure|1|desc:
After a week of labor, the runist personalizes their armor or clothing with a series of runes engraved on leather or metal pieces. They gain a DR of 1. At rank 4 in the path, the DR increases to 2.
Only the runist can benefit from their armor's magical effects and they can only enchant one armor at a time: enchanting a new armor exhausts the effectiveness of previously engraved runes.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The armor benefits from an additional +1 DEF bonus.
Voie de la gravure|2|desc:
The runist possesses a small chest whose opening is dotted with engraved runes, providing access via a dimensional portal to a much larger chest elsewhere (at the PC's home, for example).
They can store up to 10 kg per rank reached in the path of equipment while the chest remains stable at approximately 1 kg. The runist can thus retrieve from the chest any previously stored item — practically any non-perishable object, maximum 10 kg and no more than 25 cm in circumference.

*The remote chest during play
The objects stored in the chest belong to the PC, meaning they must have been deposited there beforehand. In practice — and unless the PC uses the remote chest to hide then use an object acquired during the session — rather than listing all the objects the PC might have planned in advance, it is simpler to have what they need appear when they need it.
However, the market value of objects should be deducted directly from the PC's savings to simulate the fact that before being stored in the chest, the object had to be purchased.*
Voie de la gravure|3|desc: The runist strikes the ground with their staff and causes a devastating shockwave in a 10-meter radius around them. All creatures in the area automatically take [2d6 + INT Mod.] DMG and must succeed at a STR test difficulty [10 + INT Mod.] to avoid falling to the ground and being knocked prone.
Voie de la gravure|4|desc:
The runist has created in a secure location known only to them a large stone arch engraved with complex runes. Once per day, after approximately one hour of ritual, they can trace a runic circle on the ground or a wall to create a teleportation portal allowing them to return to the stone arch's location — regardless of the distance separating them from the arch.
The anchor remains stable for a few seconds, long enough to allow other creatures — such as the PC's allies — to use the portal.
Voie de la calligraphie|0|desc:
Each day, the runist can inscribe unstable runes on [rank reached in the path x 2] scrolls that remain active for 24 hours. By crumpling the scroll, they can trigger its immediate or delayed ignition by up to one hour (to be defined at the time of handling). Any target in contact with the burning scroll takes [1d6 + INT Mod.] fire DMG.
The scrolls inflict [2d6 + INT Mod.] fire DMG at rank 4 in the path.
In combat, the runist can apply a scroll to a target in melee range with a melee attack, or throw a crumpled scroll as a ball using a ranged attack up to a range of 6 m.
**==Advanced Ability:==** Unstable scrolls cost no MP.
Voie de la calligraphie|1|desc:
After a 5-minute ritual, the runist applies a complex rune on a target's skin (or their own), active for 24 h — limited to one per creature. It allows re-rolling a d20 once per combat on an attack roll, STR, DEX, or CON test.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The rune's magic can be consumed as a free action to instantly regain [1d4 + Rank] HP. A character cannot benefit from the effects of a new energy rune for 24 hours.
Voie de la calligraphie|2|desc: After a five-minute ritual, the runist applies a series of runes on armor, active for 24 h. These allow ignoring the DMG from one attack once per combat.
Voie de la calligraphie|3|desc: After a five-minute ritual, the runist applies a rune on a weapon, active for 24 h. It allows inflicting maximum DMG, without needing to roll the die, once per combat.
Voie de la calligraphie|4|desc:
The runist draws a luminous rune on the ground (visible for the duration of the spell) and traps an area from 2 to 10 m in diameter. Any creature (of at least very small size) that enters the zone triggers an effect chosen from three (to be defined when the spell is cast):
● **==Alarm:==** a powerful gong sounds.
● **==Fire:==** [3d6 + INT Mod.] fire DMG.
● **==Fear:==** WIS test difficulty [15 + INT Mod.] or flee for 2d6 turns.
Each time a creature enters the zone again, it suffers the effects. The spell lasts 1d6 minutes but can be extended up to 12 hours through a 10-minute ritual. Creatures present in the zone during the ritual are immune to its effects.
Additionally, if this spell is cast while another ward rune is still active somewhere, the previous one immediately ends.
Voie éthérée|0|desc:
The runist gains mastery of one apprentice "attribute" glyph.
**==Advanced Ability:==** The runist gains a +2 bonus to magical attack rolls for ethereal contact spells.
Voie éthérée|1|desc:
The runist randomly draws runes from their bag to create a spell following this method: they roll 1d10 for elements and 1 to 4 d12 for attributes as the PC chooses — on doubles, the die is rerolled.
They then compare the results obtained to the glyph table and attempt to create a spell with the drawn attributes — which are all considered mastered (even the "element" glyph) — a chaotic spell therefore costs no MP.
If they roll a 0 on the element draw, the runist draws a white rune that allows them to designate the element of their choice.
If they cannot create a spell or the draw is unsatisfactory, the attempt fails and the PC has wasted their turn suffering a backlash causing them to lose [2 HP / "attribute" glyph drawn]. Instead of HP loss, the GM may decide to apply a chaotic effect to the spell escaping the runist's control related to the drawn or chosen element.
**==Advanced Ability:==** On backlash, the PC suffers a loss of [1 HP / "attribute" glyph drawn].
Voie éthérée|2|desc: The runist gains mastery of one initiate "attribute" glyph.
Voie éthérée|3|desc: The PC can recite glyphs and thus create spells without their rune bag or without having their hands free. However, they can only apply a maximum of 2 attribute glyphs with this method.
Voie éthérée|4|desc: The runist gains mastery of one adept "attribute" glyph.
Voie des runes divines|0|desc:
The runist can now use divine glyphs. Each spell can only contain one divine glyph, regardless of its rank.
**[Spell]** = 1 **[Element]** Glyph + 1-3 **[Attribute]** Glyph(s) + (1 **[Divine]** Glyph - optional)

Note: Divine glyphs are not attribute glyphs, so they cannot be improved by the catalyst glyph.
Voie des runes divines|1|desc: The runist can increase elemental DMG by one category if they wish (d4 -> d6 -> d8 -> d10).
Voie des runes divines|2|desc:
The runist gains mastery of an additional glyph, to be chosen from element, attribute, or divine glyphs.
Additionally, they can create rank 6 spells.
Voie des runes divines|3|desc: The runist can now create ethereal spells at the cost of an attack action instead of a limited action, but still cannot apply the effects of concentration.
Voie des runes divines|4|desc:
The runist gains mastery of an additional glyph, to be chosen from element, attribute, or divine glyphs.
Additionally, they can create rank 7 spells and can add one additional attribute glyph to their spells.
**[Spell] **= **1** **[Element]** Glyph + **1-4** **[Attribute]** Glyph(s) + (1 **[Divine]** Glyph - optional)
Voie des pierres esprits|0|desc:
The runist is initiated into the secret — and sometimes controversial — art of creating spirit stones, these mysterious gems capable of absorbing the soul of a deceased creature. The gem can then be set into a war golem, granting it a certain intelligence and behavior dependent on the spirit.
The golem's INT, WIS, and CHA scores become -1 and it is now capable of following simple orders consisting of a short sentence (attack all humans wearing red, wait for dawn then join me at the castle, prevent anyone from entering the house except me and my companions).
To load a spirit into a stone, the runist must paint a glyph on a creature's forehead using approximately one minute of ritual. Upon the creature's death, its spirit immediately enters the stone. The runist can only maintain one spirit enclosed in a stone.
The destruction of the war golem (HP = 0) results in the destruction of the spirit stone. Crafting a stone is a complex process requiring one hour of work.
Voie des pierres esprits|1|desc: The runist can capture and keep up to two spirits. They can change the spirit stone in their golem through a quick but delicate operation requiring ten minutes of work.
Voie des pierres esprits|2|desc: The stone's structure is more complex and manages to better convey the creature's spirit. The war golem gains the stone's base ability.
Voie des pierres esprits|3|desc:
The golem gains autonomy and can take initiatives that seem right to it in the runist's interest. If the runist's HP drops to 0, the spirit stone explodes and immediately transfers the golem's remaining HP to the runist (up to the PC's max HP).
The runist can capture and keep up to three spirits.
Voie des pierres esprits|4|desc:
Rank 5: Perfect Spirit Stone
The runist has managed to create perfect spirit stones. The war golem gains the stone's advanced ability in addition to the base ability.
